  equity_percentage decimal [note: 'The percentage equity stake held by this account in the Joint Venture, as defined in the JV agreement. Belongs to the participation record because the same account may hold different equity stakes in different JVs, and the same JV has multiple participants with different stakes.']
  exit_date date [note: 'The date on which this account exited or was bought out of the Joint Venture, if applicable. Null if the account remains an active participant. Belongs to the participation record as exit terms are negotiated per participant.']
  is_lead_sponsor boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this participation record represents the lead sponsor of the JV. Replaces the is_lead_sponsor_internal flag on jv_structure for per-participant modelling. At most one participation record per JV should have this set to true.']
  is_lead_sponsor_internal boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the lead sponsor of the JV is an internal entity (i.e., Construction itself or one of its subsidiaries) as opposed to an external third-party organisation. Drives internal vs. external reporting segmentation. [Moved from jv_structure: This boolean on jv_structure indicates whether the lead sponsor is an internal entity. With the association table, this property belongs to the specific participation record of the lead sponsor account, not to the JV structure as a whole. It should move to the association as a derivable flag or be captured via the account\'s own internal/external classification.]']
  join_date date [note: 'The date on which this account formally joined the Joint Venture, as recorded in the JV agreement or a subsequent deed of accession. Belongs to the participation record as it is specific to each account-JV combination.']
  participation_role varchar [note: 'The formal role of this account within the Joint Venture as defined in the JV agreement. Determines governance rights, liability exposure, and management responsibilities. Belongs to the participation record as the same account may hold different roles in different JVs.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the formal Participation role between a jv_structure and an account. It captures each client organisation\'s legally binding membership in a Joint Venture arrangement, including their equity stake, committed capital, role, and tenure. Each record links one jv_structure to one participating account with attributes that exist only in the context of that specific participation — data that is neither a property of the JV structure alone nor of the account alone.. Existence Justification: In the construction industry, Joint Ventures are formal multi-party legal arrangements where multiple client organizations (accounts) co-own and co-sponsor a project. A single JV structure has many participating accounts (partners), and a single account can participate in many JV structures across different projects over time. This is an operationally managed relationship — JV participation agreements are actively created, amended, and dissolved as part of project delivery governance, with each participation record carrying legally significant financial data (equity stake, committed capital, role).'
}

Table contract.agreement {
  agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the master contract record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client (owner) of the contract.']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Contract administration requires identifying the named client-side representative (e.g., Employer\'s Representative or Contract Administrator) for each agreement. This contact receives notices, certifi']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project that the contract supports.']
  framework_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_framework_agreement. Business justification: Call-off contract management: construction agreements are frequently executed as call-offs under a client framework agreement. Contract managers and commercial teams must trace which framework agreeme']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary contractor responsible for delivering the work.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the agreement.']
  amendment_effective_date date [note: 'Date the amendment becomes effective.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential identifier for a contract amendment.']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Type of amendment (e.g., supplemental, variation, novation).']
  award_date date [note: 'Date the contract was formally awarded to the contractor.']
  boq_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Bill of Quantities associated with the contract.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory and statutory compliance obligations attached to the agreement.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External contract identifier used in all business communications and legal documents.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the agreement (e.g., EPC, GMP, Lump Sum, Unit Rate, Design-Build, Design-Bid-Build, PPP, BOT). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EPC|GMP|LumpSum|UnitRate|DesignBuild|DesignBidBuild|PPP|BOT — promote to reference product]']
  contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original monetary value of the agreement as agreed at award.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the contract value.']
  defects_liability_period_months varchar [note: 'Duration after completion during which the contractor is liable for defects.']
  dispute_resolution varchar [note: 'Method for resolving disputes (e.g., arbitration, litigation, mediation).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date the agreement terminates or expires (null for open‑ended contracts).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date the agreement becomes legally binding.']
  environmental_impact_assessment_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved environmental impact assessment linked to the contract.']
  extension_of_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days granted as an extension of time (EOT) under the contract.']
  geographic_location varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the contract work is performed.']
  governing_law varchar [note: 'Jurisdiction whose law governs the contract.']
  health_safety_plan_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the HSE plan governing safety on the contract site.']
  insurance_requirements varchar [note: 'Required insurance coverage and limits for the contract.']
  liquidated_damages_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount payable for each day of delay beyond the agreed completion date.']
  milestone_schedule varchar [note: 'Key project milestones linked to contract payments.']
  notice_to_proceed_date date [note: 'Date the client issued the Notice to Proceed (NTP) to start work.']
  payment_schedule varchar [note: 'Description of payment milestones, dates, and conditions.']
  performance_bond_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Security amount posted by the contractor to guarantee performance.']
  retention_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of each payment retained until contract close‑out.']
  revised_completion_date date [note: 'New contract completion date after amendment.']
  revised_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contract value after applying the amendment.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the work scope covered by the agreement.']
  special_conditions varchar [note: 'Any special or bespoke conditions that modify standard contract terms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contract record.']
  wbs_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the contract to the associated Work Breakdown Structure.']

  Note: 'Master contract record representing the authoritative SSOT for all contractual agreements in the construction domain. Captures FIDIC-based EPC, GMP (Guaranteed Maximum Price), lump-sum, unit-rate, DB (Design-Build), DBB (Design-Bid-Build), PPP (Public-Private Partnership), and BOT (Build-Operate-Transfer) contract types via a type classification attribute. Stores contract identity, scope narrative, detailed scope of works including WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) reference, deliverable descriptions, technical specifications reference, BOQ (Bill of Quantities) linkage, geographic boundaries, and exclusions. Records execution dates, NTP (Notice to Proceed) date, contract value, currency, governing law, dispute resolution mechanism, contract status lifecycle from award through close-out, and particular/special conditions that modify standard form terms. Maintains status transition history for audit and lifecycle analytics. Records all formal amendments including supplemental agreements, deed of variations, and novation agreements with amendment number, type, effective date, revised contract value, revised completion date, and execution status. Serves as the authoritative scope baseline against which change orders and EOT claims are evaluated. Canonical contract.agreement entity (v2 curated).'
}

Table contract.party {
  party_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each party associated with a contract.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: Required for Contract Administration Report linking each contract party to the client account it represents, enabling account‑level performance and compliance tracking.']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Needed for Communication Log and Regulatory Contact Register to identify the primary client contact responsible for a contract party.']
  jv_structure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.jv_structure. Business justification: JV contract party management: when a JV entity is a named party to a contract, the party record must reference the governing JV structure to enforce profit-sharing, liability limits, and participation']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Maps each contract party that is a supplier to its vendor record, enabling vendor performance tracking per contract.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the party.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Additional address information (suite, floor, etc.).']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Account number used for contract‑related payments.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Financial institution where the party\'s account is held.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the mailing address.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Main email used for contractual communications.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for reaching the party.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code for the mailing address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the party record was first entered into the system.']
  insurance_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the insurance policy covering the party.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the provided insurance coverage ends.']
  is_jv_partner boolean [note: 'True when the party participates as a JV partner in the contract.']
  is_primary_party boolean [note: 'True when the party is the main responsible entity for the contract.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code where the party is legally registered.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Official registered name of the corporate entity.']
  liability_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary cap on the party\'s contractual liability.']
  name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the party (individual or organization) as it appears in the contract.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments, observations, or special conditions related to the party.']
  participation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ownership or profit share for joint‑venture partners.']
  party_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the party within the contract management system.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Category that defines the business role of the party in construction contracts.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed payment schedule (e.g., Net 30).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the mailing address.']
  record_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that originated the party data.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued registration identifier for the legal entity.']
  role_in_contract varchar [note: 'The functional role the party plays for the specific contract (e.g., owner, contractor).']
  signatory_authority boolean [note: 'True when the party is authorized to execute the contract on behalf of its organization.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the mailing address.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax ID for the party.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the latest modification to the party record.']

  Note: 'Association entity capturing all parties bound to a contract agreement, including the employer/client, general contractor (GC), joint venture (JV) partners, engineer/consultant, and guarantors. Records party role, signatory authority, legal entity name, registration number, jurisdiction, and participation percentage for JV arrangements. Enables multi-party contract structures common in EPC and PPP delivery models. Canonical contract.party entity (v2 curated).'
}

Table contract.scope {

  longitude double [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the primary site for the scope.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the scope of works.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the scope.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Planned completion date for the scope.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date for execution of the scope.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the total quantity (e.g., cubic meters, kilograms).']
  region varchar [note: 'Region or state within the country for the scope location.']

  scope_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scope.']

  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier (e.g., v1.0, v2.1) for the scope.']
  wbs_reference varchar [note: 'Human‑readable WBS code or description.']

  Note: 'Defines the detailed scope of works attached to a contract agreement, including WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) reference, deliverable descriptions, technical specifications reference, BOQ (Bill of Quantities) linkage, geographic boundaries, and exclusions. Serves as the authoritative scope baseline against which change orders and EOT (Extension of Time) claims are evaluated. Canonical contract.contract_scope entity (v2 curated).'
}

Table contract.contract_milestone {
  contract_milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the contract milestone record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent contract to which this milestone belongs.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project associated with the contract milestone.']
  drawing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.drawing. Business justification: Contract milestones such as \'Issue For Construction drawing package\' or \'Design Freeze\' are directly tied to specific drawings. Project controls teams track milestone achievement against drawing issue']
  project_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to single source of truth project.project_milestone (SSOT dedup).']
  responsible_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (e.g., contractor, project manager) responsible for delivering the milestone.']
  scope_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_scope. Business justification: Contract milestones are defined within the context of a specific scope of work. A scope item (e.g., civil works, MEP installation) has associated contractual milestones (e.g., completion of foundation']
  actual_date date [note: 'Date the milestone was actually achieved or certified.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of any amendment made to the milestone.']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for the most recent amendment to the milestone.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates compliance of the milestone with contractual and regulatory requirements.']
  contract_milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the milestone.']
  cost_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between milestone_value and amount actually paid.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the milestone record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the milestone value.']
  defects_notified_date date [note: 'Date the employer formally notified the contractor of defects.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional context for the milestone.']
  dlp_end_date date [note: 'End date of the Defects Liability Period.']
  dlp_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the Defects Liability Period associated with the milestone.']
  final_certificate_issued_date date [note: 'Date the final completion certificate was issued.']
  forecast_date date [note: 'Updated forecasted date based on progress and risk assessments.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the milestone is deemed critical for project schedule or payment.']
  ld_rate_per_day decimal(10,2) [note: 'Daily rate applied if liquidated damages are triggered.']
  ld_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether liquidated damages have been incurred for this milestone.']
  liquidated_damages_applicable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether liquidated damages may be assessed for this milestone.']
  milestone_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the milestone, used in contracts and payment schedules.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the milestone (e.g., Notice to Proceed, Practical Completion).']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the milestone type according to contract terminology.']
  milestone_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the milestone, often linked to a payment certificate.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments entered by users.']
  outstanding_defects_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any defects remain unresolved at DLP expiry.']
  payment_certificate_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Date the payment certificate was issued.']
  payment_certificate_number decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reference number of the payment certificate linked to the milestone.']
  performance_certificate_date date [note: 'Date the performance certificate was issued.']
  performance_certificate_issued boolean [note: 'Flag showing if a performance certificate has been issued for the milestone.']
  planned_date date [note: 'Date originally scheduled for the milestone in the contract baseline.']
  rectification_deadline date [note: 'Deadline by which the contractor must rectify notified defects.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to specific regulatory clause or standard governing the milestone (e.g., FIDIC Clause 12.1).']
  retention_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the milestone value retained until final acceptance.']
  retention_release_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for releasing retained amounts after final acceptance.']
  schedule_variance_days varchar [note: 'Difference in days between planned_date and actual_date.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the milestone record.']

  Note: 'Tracks contractually obligated milestones, key dates, and DLP (Defects Liability Period) obligations within a contract. Covers NTP (Notice to Proceed), sectional completion dates, practical completion, DLP start/end dates, performance certificate issuance, and final certificate dates. Records planned date, forecast date, actual achieved date, milestone value (if linked to payment), milestone type, and milestone status. Manages the complete DLP register including defects notified by the employer, contractor rectification commitments, rectification deadlines, outstanding defects at DLP expiry, and the transition from practical completion through to final certificate issuance and retention release. Used for LD (Liquidated Damages) trigger assessment, payment certification, DLP management, and retention release scheduling. Canonical contract.contract_milestone entity (v2 curated). SSOT: authoritative source is project.project_milestone.'
}

Table contract.payment_schedule {
  payment_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the payment schedule.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract to which this payment schedule belongs.']
  contract_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the milestone associated with the payment.']
  actual_payment_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Date the payment was actually made.']
  advance_balance_remaining decimal(18,2) [note: 'Outstanding advance amount yet to be recovered.']
  advance_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of the advance payment disbursed.']
  advance_payment_flag decimal(18,2) [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule includes an advance payment component.']
  advance_recovery_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount recovered from subsequent payments.']
  business_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp representing the business event that generated the schedule, e.g., milestone approval.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment schedule record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the payment amounts.']
  description decimal(18,2) [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context for the payment schedule.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any discount applied to the gross amount before tax.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the payment is contractually due.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before taxes, discounts, or retentions.']
  guarantee_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the advance payment guarantee document.']
  installment_number varchar [note: 'Sequence number of this payment within the overall schedule.']
  is_final_payment decimal(18,2) [note: 'True if this schedule represents the final payment of the contract.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the payment schedule.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount payable after tax, discount, and retention adjustments.']
  net_amount_after_discount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net payable amount after discount and before tax.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or observations related to the payment schedule.']
  payment_certificate_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Date the payment certificate was issued.']
  payment_certificate_number decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reference number of the payment certificate issued for this schedule.']
  payment_method decimal(18,2) [note: 'Means by which the payment will be transferred.']
  payment_method_reference decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reference details for the payment method, such as bank account number.']
  payment_source decimal(18,2) [note: 'Originating party responsible for the payment.']
  payment_status decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current processing status of the payment.']
  payment_terms decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual terms governing payment timing, e.g., Net 30.']
  payment_type decimal(18,2) [note: 'Classification of the payment schedule based on its nature.']
  retention_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount retained from the payment as per contract terms.']
  retention_release_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of retention to be released on the scheduled release date.']
  retention_release_date date [note: 'Date when the retained amount is scheduled to be released.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the payment schedule as defined in the contract.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Date the payment is scheduled to be processed.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the gross amount.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage for the payment.']
  total_installments varchar [note: 'Total number of installments defined in the contract.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment schedule record.']

  Note: 'Defines the contractual payment schedule governing when and how much the employer is obligated to pay the contractor, including full advance payment (mobilization payment) lifecycle management. Captures payment terms, payment intervals, payment due dates, payment method, currency, and supports both milestone-based and time-based payment structures as defined under FIDIC and GMP contract forms. Manages the complete advance payment lifecycle: advance payment amount, percentage of contract value, disbursement date, repayment/recovery schedule, cumulative amount recovered through payment certificate deductions, outstanding advance balance, advance payment guarantee reference, and advance payment status from initial disbursement through full recovery. Serves as the authoritative SSOT for all payment scheduling and advance payment tracking, alongside progress payment and retention release scheduling. Canonical contract.payment_schedule entity (v2 curated).'
}

Table contract.payment_certificate {
  payment_certificate_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the payment certificate record.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the project associated with the contract.']
  contract_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_milestone. Business justification: Payment certificates in construction are typically issued upon milestone completion (e.g., IPC tied to a progress milestone, FPC tied to the completion milestone). payment_certificate currently has mi']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.invoice. Business justification: Payment certificates in construction are matched against vendor/subcontractor invoices for AP three-way matching and certified-vs-invoiced reconciliation reporting. invoice_number and invoice_date on ']
  payment_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.payment_schedule. Business justification: A payment certificate (IPC/FPC) is the actual certified payment event that corresponds to a scheduled payment installment defined in the payment_schedule. Linking payment_certificate to payment_schedu']
  progress_billing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.progress_billing. Business justification: In construction payment workflows, a payment certificate is issued in direct response to a progress billing application. This link enables billing-to-certification reconciliation — a core contract adm']
  progress_update_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.progress_update. Business justification: Payment certification in construction is directly based on measured schedule progress. The payment certificate must reference the specific progress update snapshot used to certify the work quantum. wo']
  submittal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.design_submittal. Business justification: Payment certificates are often conditional on approved design submittals (e.g., approved shop drawings before payment for fabricated items). Contract administrators and QA teams use this link to verif']
  actual_payment_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Date on which the payment was actually received.']
  advance_recovery_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount recovered against any previously paid advances.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certificate was approved.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to each payment certificate, following the convention CP########.']
  certification_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certificate was formally issued.']
  certification_version varchar [note: 'Version number for revised certificates.']
  certified_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Gross amount certified for payment before deductions.']
  change_order_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of any change order affecting this payment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certificate record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the currency for the payment.']
  is_advance_recovered boolean [note: 'True when an advance payment is being recovered.']
  is_ld_applied boolean [note: 'True when liquidated damages are deducted.']
  is_pay_when_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'Indicates whether payment is contingent on receipt of funds from the client.']
  is_retention_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates if retention is being held for this certificate.']
  ld_deduction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Deduction applied for liquidated damages, if any.']
  net_amount_due decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final payable amount after all deductions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the certificate.']
  payment_certificate_status decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the certificate.']
  payment_due_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Date by which the net amount is contractually due.']
  payment_method decimal(18,2) [note: 'Means by which the payment will be made.']
  payment_status decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current status of the payment transaction.']
  payment_type decimal(18,2) [note: 'Indicates whether the certificate is for an interim or final payment.']
  retention_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount retained per contract terms, deducted from the certified amount.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the certified amount.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax regime applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the certificate record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each interim payment certificate (IPC) and final payment certificate (FPC) issued under a main contract or subcontract. Captures certified amount, retention deducted, advance payment recovery, LD deductions, net amount due, certification date, certifier identity, payment due date, and actual payment date. Supports both main contract certification (FIDIC Clause 14) and subcontract payment certification via a contract-level discriminator distinguishing main contract from subcontract certificates. Records subcontract payment applications, certified amounts, and enables back-to-back payment flow management between main contract receipts and subcontractor disbursements. Supports cash flow forecasting, pay-when-paid clause administration, and unified payment reconciliation across the full contract hierarchy. Canonical contract.payment_certificate entity (v2 curated).'
}

Table contract.contract_change_order {
  contract_change_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for the contract change order record.']
  cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Change orders affect specific cost codes in the project budget. Linking change orders to cost codes enables cost impact analysis by cost category and budget line — a standard construction cost control']
  drawing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.drawing. Business justification: Change orders in construction are almost always triggered by or reference a revised drawing (e.g., design change instruction). Contract administrators must link CCOs to the causative drawing for cost/']
  labor_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.labor_rate. Business justification: Change orders for time-and-material or changed-conditions work require referencing the applicable labor rate schedule to calculate cost impact. Construction cost engineers and contract administrators ']
  rfi_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.rfi. Business justification: RFI responses with cost or schedule impact are a primary source of change orders. Linking CCO to the originating RFI is standard change management practice; contract administrators use this for variat']
  scope_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_scope. Business justification: A change order (CO/variation) is issued against a specific scope of work defined in contract_scope. contract_change_order currently has scope_description (STRING) as a denormalized text field. Adding ']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contractor or subcontractor responsible for executing the change order work.']
  work_front_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.work_front. Business justification: Change orders frequently affect a particular work front; the link supports impact analysis per front.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of adjustments (e.g., taxes, fees, discounts) applied to the gross amount.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or authority that approved the change order.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the change order received approval.']
  change_order_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the change order by the contract administration system.']
  change_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the change order purpose, e.g., addition, omission, time extension, price adjustment, or scope change.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks entered by users regarding the change order.']
  contract_change_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the change order.']
  cost_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary impact of the change order on the contract price (positive for additions, negative for omissions).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the change order record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for monetary values.']
  dlp_end_date date [note: 'Date when the Defects Liability Period expires for work covered by the change order.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the change order becomes contractually effective.']
  eot_claim_reference varchar [note: 'Link to any Extension of Time claim associated with the schedule impact.']
  executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the change order was executed and became binding.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before any adjustments, taxes, or discounts.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the change order is considered critical to project delivery.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the change order record.']
  ld_provision_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount stipulated for liquidated damages if the change order affects performance penalties.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final payable amount after adjustments.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the change order was initiated.']
  revised_completion_date date [note: 'New contract completion date after applying schedule impact.']
  schedule_impact_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the contract schedule is extended or reduced due to the change order.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the change order was formally submitted for approval.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the change order record.']
  variation_instruction_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the original variation instruction that triggered this change order.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the change order record.']

  Note: 'Authoritative log of all CO (Change Orders) and contract variations issued against a contract. Records variation instruction reference, scope description, reason code (employer-directed, unforeseen conditions, design change), cost impact (addition or omission), schedule impact in days, approval status, and executed value. Tracks the full variation lifecycle from instruction through valuation, negotiation, and formal execution. Links to EOT claims where schedule impact is asserted. Canonical contract.contract_change_order entity (v2 curated).'
}

Table contract.eot_claim {
  eot_claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for the EOT claim record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying contract to which this EOT claim relates.']
  claim_related_ncr_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a Non‑Conformance Report linked to the claim.']
  claim_related_rfi_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a Request for Information that is associated with the claim.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project associated with the claim.']
  contract_change_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a change order that may be linked to the time extension request.']
  contract_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_milestone. Business justification: An EOT (Extension of Time) claim directly asserts entitlement to extend a specific contractual milestone date. contract_eot_claim has claim_impact_on_milestones (STRING), claim_original_schedule_date,']
  daily_log_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.daily_log. Business justification: EOT claims must be substantiated by contemporaneous daily log records evidencing the delay event (weather, access denial, force majeure). Contract administrators and arbitrators require direct linkage']
  delay_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.delay_event. Business justification: EOT claim submission process requires referencing the specific schedule delay event that triggered the claim. Construction contracts mandate that EOT claims cite the causative delay event for entitlem']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contractor submitting the EOT claim.']
  safety_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.incident. Business justification: Safety incidents causing stop-work orders or LTI events are a recognised EOT claim basis in construction contracts. EOT claim assessors require the linked incident record to validate delay causation. ']
  submittal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.design_submittal. Business justification: EOT claims are frequently substantiated by delayed design submittal approvals (e.g., late approval of shop drawings causing construction delay). Claims assessors require direct traceability from EOT c']
  claim_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial value of the claim based on days claimed and daily rate.']
  claim_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who approved or rejected the claim.']
  claim_assessor_comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text comments entered by the engineer or assessor during evaluation.']
  claim_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the claim record was first created in the system.']
  claim_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the claim amount.']
  claim_decision_date date [note: 'Date when the final decision on the claim was recorded.']
  claim_external_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number used by external parties (e.g., client or auditor) to identify the claim.']
  claim_final_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final monetary amount after any adjustments or partial approvals.']
  claim_final_days varchar [note: 'Total number of days finally approved for the extension.']
  claim_is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the claim is deemed critical to project delivery or financial exposure.']
  claim_new_schedule_date date [note: 'Projected completion date after applying the approved extension.']
  claim_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured by any stakeholder.']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'Unique claim number assigned by the contractor or system for tracking.']
  claim_original_schedule_date date [note: 'Baseline project completion date before any extensions.']
  claim_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the claim for processing urgency.']
  claim_resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the claim was fully closed and any adjustments were posted.']
  claim_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the claim was formally reviewed by the project engineer.']
  claim_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system where the claim was first recorded.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the claim within the claim lifecycle.']
  claim_status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the current status, such as reason for rejection or hold.']
  claim_submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contractor formally submitted the EOT claim.']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the contractual basis for the time extension request.']
  claim_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the claim record.']
  days_assessed varchar [note: 'Number of days approved by the engineer after assessment.']
  days_claimed varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the contractor claims as an extension.']
  determination_outcome varchar [note: 'Final decision result after engineering review.']
  entitlement_basis varchar [note: 'FIDIC sub‑clause or contractual provision that justifies the claim.']
  liquidated_damages_impact decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated reduction in liquidated damages exposure due to the approved extension.']
  revised_completion_date date [note: 'New projected project completion date after the approved extension.']
  supporting_document_refs varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of document IDs (e.g., drawings, logs, photos) that substantiate the claim.']

  Note: 'Records EOT (Extension of Time) claims submitted by the contractor asserting entitlement to additional time due to employer risk events, force majeure, or other contractual grounds. Captures claim submission date, delay event description, days claimed, days assessed by engineer, grounds of entitlement (FIDIC Sub-Clause reference), supporting documentation references, and final determination outcome. Critical for managing LD (Liquidated Damages) exposure and revised completion date. Canonical contract.contract_eot_claim entity (v2 curated).'
}

Business justification: A performance bond or guarantee is issued by or on behalf of a specific contractual party (typically the contractor or a JV partner). bond_guarantee currently links to agreement and vendor (cross-doma']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Connects bond guarantee issuing party to vendor master, required for bond compliance and risk reporting.']
  bond_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the bond guarantee.']

  call_date date [note: 'Date when the bond was called, if applicable.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bond guarantee record was created in the system.']

  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the bond guarantee expires.']

  release_date date [note: 'Date when the bond was released or returned to the issuer.']
  retention_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the contract value retained under the bond, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bond guarantee record.']

  Note: 'Tracks all performance bonds, advance payment guarantees, retention bonds, bid bonds, and parent company guarantees associated with a contract. Records bond type, issuing bank or surety, bond amount, bond currency, issue date, expiry date, call conditions, and current status (active, expired, called, released). Provides the authoritative bond register for contract administration and financial risk management. Canonical contract.bond_guarantee entity (v2 curated).'
}

Table contract.insurance_register {
  insurance_register_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the insurance register record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Insurance policies in the insurance_register are contractually required per agreement (the contract specifies insurance requirements). Currently insurance_register only links to party via insured_part']
  insured_party_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the contract or entity that is insured.']
  claim_history_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any claims have been filed under this policy.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the policy meets contractual and regulatory requirements.']
  coverage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount the policy will pay for covered losses.']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Category of insurance coverage required by the contract.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the insurance register entry was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  deductible_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount the insured party must pay before the insurer covers losses.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the insurance coverage becomes active.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the insurance coverage terminates unless renewed.']
  insurance_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the issued insurance certificate document.']
  insurance_register_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the insurance policy.']
  insurer_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the insurance company providing the coverage.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the country where the policy is governed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the policy.']
  policy_document_reference varchar [note: 'Location or identifier of the stored insurance certificate/document.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned by the insurer to the insurance policy.']
  premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Periodic payment amount required to keep the policy in force.']
  premium_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the premium payment.']
  premium_due_date date [note: 'Date the next premium payment must be received.']
  premium_payment_frequency decimal(18,2) [note: 'How often the premium is due.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the policy satisfies all applicable regulatory requirements (OSHA, ISO, etc.).']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date by which the policy must be renewed to avoid lapse.']
  renewal_notification_sent boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if a renewal reminder has been sent to the contract administrator.']
  risk_class varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to the policy based on project exposure.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the record.']

  Note: 'Maintains the register of all contractually required insurance policies for a contract, including contractor all-risk (CAR), third-party liability, professional indemnity, workers compensation, and marine cargo. Records policy number, insurer name, coverage type, coverage amount, policy period, deductible, and compliance status. Tracks insurance renewal obligations and notifies contract administrators of expiring coverage. Canonical contract.insurance_register entity (v2 curated).'
}

Table contract.subcontract {
  subcontract_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for the subcontract record.']
  cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Subcontracts in construction are assigned to specific cost codes for budget tracking, committed cost reporting, and cost-to-complete analysis. Every construction cost controller expects subcontracts t']
  hse_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.hse_plan. Business justification: Subcontracts in construction contractually require a governing HSE plan. Subcontractor HSE compliance audits and regulatory inspections depend on this link. hse_plan.subcontractor_hse_requirements con']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to material.material_master. Business justification: REQUIRED: Subcontracts specify the exact material to be supplied; this FK supports the Subcontract‑Material tracking sheet used in procurement.']
  scope_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_scope. Business justification: A subcontract is let to execute a specific portion of the main contract\'s scope of work. subcontract currently has scope_summary (STRING) as a denormalized text description. Adding contract_scope_id →']
  technical_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.technical_specification. Business justification: Subcontracts are issued against specific technical specifications defining workmanship and material standards. Contract administrators must verify subcontract compliance against the governing spec. A ']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Links subcontract vendor to master vendor for compliance, insurance, and bonding checks.']
  change_order_count varchar [note: 'Total number of change orders issued against this subcontract.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Target date for subcontract completion as per the contract.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the subcontract with regulatory and contractual obligations.']
  contract_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the subcontract agreement (e.g., Engineering Procurement Construction, Guaranteed Maximum Price, Lump‑Sum).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subcontract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the subcontract value.']
  defects_liability_period_end date [note: 'Date when the defects liability period expires.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the subcontract becomes legally binding (often the Notice to Proceed date).']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the subcontract ends or expires; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  extension_of_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days granted as an extension of time for project completion.']
  insurance_requirements varchar [note: 'Summary of insurance coverage obligations for the subcontractor.']
  liquidated_damages_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty amount stipulated for delays or breaches.']
  milestone_description varchar [note: 'Key contractual milestones associated with the subcontract.']

  payment_schedule_description decimal(18,2) [note: 'Textual description of the payment milestones and dates.']
  payment_terms decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed for the subcontract.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative risk rating assigned to the subcontract.']
  subcontract_number varchar [note: 'External contract number or code assigned to the subcontract as defined in the main contract documents.']
  subcontract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the subcontract.']
  subcontract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the subcontract based on procurement approach (e.g., domestic, nominated, specialist, trade).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the subcontract record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the subcontract record.']
  value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the subcontract as agreed in the contract.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the subcontract record.']

  Note: 'Master record for subcontracts let by the main contractor to specialist trade and MEP (Mechanical Electrical Plumbing) subcontractors under the main contract. Captures subcontract type (domestic, nominated, specialist), subcontract value, scope summary, back-to-back terms with main contract, NTP date, completion date, and subcontract status. Distinct from the subcontractor domain master — this entity owns the contractual instrument, not the subcontractor party profile. Canonical contract.subcontract entity (v2 curated).'
}

Business justification: Subcontract payments must be reconciled against actual bank payment records for AP close-out and audit compliance. This link enables the subcontract payment reconciliation report — a standard construc']

Linking subcontract_payment to the specific progress_update snapshot supports subcontractor payment certification, dispute reso']

Without a FK to subcontract, there is no direct structural link between a payment and the subcontract it belongs to ']
  vendor_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_invoice. Business justification: Subcontract payments are made against vendor invoices submitted by subcontractors. Linking subcontract_payment to vendor_invoice enables payment-to-invoice matching for subcontract accounts payable re']
  actual_payment_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Date the payment was actually received by the subcontractor.']

  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the payment is contractually due.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount claimed before any deductions.']

  retention_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the payment retained per contract terms.']

  subcontract_payment_status decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payment record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the payment record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of payment applications and payment certificates issued under each subcontract. Captures application amount, certified amount, retention deducted, LD deductions, net payment due, payment due date, and actual payment date. Enables back-to-back payment flow management between main contract receipts and subcontractor disbursements, supporting cash flow forecasting and pay-when-paid clause administration. Canonical contract.subcontract_payment entity (v2 curated).'
}

Table site.work_front {
  work_front_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the work front record. Primary key for the work_front master entity in the site domain.']
  bim_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.bim_model. Business justification: Work fronts are spatially coordinated against BIM models for clash detection and 4D scheduling. BIM-based work front management is standard practice; \'bim_model_reference\' is a plain-text denormalizat']
  drawing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.drawing. Business justification: Work fronts are spatially defined by construction drawings (grid references, zone boundaries, structural elements). Site engineers assign drawing packages to work fronts for scope definition and IFC c']
  finance_cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Cost allocation reports require each work front to reference the central cost code for budgeting and variance analysis.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_party. Business justification: Each work front is assigned a primary contract party (sub‑contractor or client) for responsibility, invoicing and performance tracking.']
  primary_work_construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent project under which this work front is executed. Enables roll-up of work front data to project-level reporting and earned value computation.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.site. Business justification: A work front belongs to a single construction site; adding work_front.site_id creates the required parent relationship and eliminates site isolation.']
  subcontract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.subcontract. Business justification: Work fronts are executed under specific subcontract contracts; linking enables schedule, cost and risk integration.']
  swms_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.swms. Business justification: Each work front requires a governing SWMS before work commences — a mandatory WHS regulatory requirement. HSE auditors verify SWMS coverage per work front. \'swms_reference\' is a denormalized text fiel']
  access_restriction varchar [note: 'The access control classification for this work front. \'permit_required\' indicates a Permit to Work (PTW) is mandatory before entry; \'restricted_zone\' limits access to authorised personnel; \'exclusion_zone\' prohibits all non-essential access. Supports HSE compliance.']
  actual_crew_size varchar [note: 'The actual number of direct labour workers present at this work front on the most recent reporting day. Compared against planned_crew_size to identify under-manning and productivity risks.']
  actual_production_qty decimal(14,3) [note: 'Cumulative actual production quantity achieved at this work front to date, expressed in the production_unit. Sourced from HCSS HeavyJob production tracking and field measurements. Used to compute earned value.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'The actual date on which work commenced at this work front. Compared against planned_start_date to compute schedule variance. Sourced from Procore daily logs or HCSS HeavyJob time tracking.']
  area_sqm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total plan area of the work front in square metres. Used as the principal quantitative measure of the resource for production rate calculations, crew density planning, and progress measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this work front record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  demobilization_date date [note: 'The date on which all resources were demobilized from this work front and active work ceased. Null if the work front is still active. Used to calculate work front duration and close out cost codes.']
  elevation_m decimal(8,3) [note: 'Reference elevation of the work front above mean sea level in metres. Critical for earthworks volume calculations, drainage design, and structural level control.']
  environmental_sensitivity varchar [note: 'Classification of the environmental sensitivity of the work front location. \'sensitive\' and \'protected\' areas require additional environmental controls and monitoring per ISO 14001 and EPA requirements. \'remediation_required\' indicates contaminated ground conditions.']
  forecast_finish_date date [note: 'Current forecast date for completion of all work at this work front, updated based on actual progress and remaining work. Supports look-ahead scheduling and early warning of delays.']
  front_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the work front within the project or site. Used in daily logs, production reports, and cost coding in HCSS HeavyJob and Procore.']
  front_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the work front (e.g., \'Zone A – Pile Cap Excavation\', \'North Abutment Face\'). Used in daily logs, progress reports, and field management interfaces.']
  front_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the work front. \'mobilizing\' indicates resources are being deployed; \'active\' indicates concurrent work is in progress; \'suspended\' indicates a temporary hold; \'demobilized\' indicates resources have been withdrawn; \'completed\' indicates all planned work is finished.']
  front_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the spatial unit represented by this work front. Distinguishes between zone (broad area), area (defined boundary), face (excavation or structural face), sector, corridor, level (floor/elevation), or bay. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: zone|area|face|sector|corridor|level|bay — promote to reference product if additional types are required]']
  grid_reference varchar [note: 'Site-specific grid reference or chainage notation (e.g., \'Grid E5-F6\', \'CH 1+200 to CH 1+450\') used in engineering drawings and BIM models to locate the work front. Complements GPS coordinates for internal site navigation.']
  heavyjob_cost_center varchar [note: 'The cost center identifier in HCSS HeavyJob associated with this work front. Used for field-level cost allocation, equipment hour tracking, and production rate reporting.']
  hse_risk_level varchar [note: 'The assessed HSE risk level for this work front based on the activities being performed, proximity to hazards, and environmental conditions. Drives frequency of safety inspections and toolbox meetings (TBM) requirements.']
  is_critical_path boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work front contains activities on the project critical path as determined by the CPM schedule in Oracle Primavera P6. Critical path work fronts receive priority resource allocation and daily progress monitoring.']
  is_subcontracted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work at this work front is executed by a subcontractor rather than the general contractor\'s own workforce. Drives subcontractor management workflows and contract administration processes.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this work front record was most recently modified. Used for incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer and audit trail maintenance.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate of the work front centroid or reference point in decimal degrees (WGS84). Enables spatial aggregation and GIS-based reporting of production and progress data.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate of the work front centroid or reference point in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used alongside latitude for spatial mapping and IoT sensor integration.']
  mobilization_date date [note: 'The date on which resources (crew, equipment, materials) were first mobilized to this work front and active work commenced. Marks the start of the work front lifecycle for scheduling and cost tracking.']
  percent_complete decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current physical percent complete of all planned work at this work front, expressed as a value between 0.00 and 100.00. Sourced from field progress measurements and used as input to earned value computation in the project domain.']
  planned_crew_size varchar [note: 'The planned number of direct labour workers assigned to this work front per shift as defined in the resource-loaded schedule. Used for crew density analysis and productivity benchmarking.']
  planned_finish_date date [note: 'Baseline-scheduled finish date for all work activities at this work front as defined in the project schedule. Used for schedule performance index (SPI) calculation and look-ahead planning.']
  planned_production_qty decimal(14,3) [note: 'Total planned production quantity for this work front expressed in the production_unit. Derived from the Bill of Quantities (BOQ) and used as the budget quantity for earned value computation.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Baseline-scheduled start date for work activities at this work front as defined in the project schedule (Oracle Primavera P6). Used for schedule variance analysis and earned value computation.']
  procore_location_reference varchar [note: 'The location identifier assigned to this work front in the Procore construction management system. Used to link daily logs, RFIs, submittals, and change orders to the correct spatial location.']
  production_unit varchar [note: 'The primary unit of measure used to track production output at this work front (e.g., \'m3\' for earthworks, \'LM\' for piling, \'m2\' for formwork, \'tonnes\' for steel erection). Aligns with the Bill of Quantities (BOQ) unit for earned value computation.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'The shift working pattern applied at this work front. Determines daily productive hours available for scheduling and cost rate calculations.']
  weather_sensitivity varchar [note: 'Classification of how sensitive the work activities at this work front are to adverse weather conditions. \'high\' sensitivity work fronts (e.g., concrete pours, earthworks) require weather monitoring and contingency planning.']
  zone_classification varchar [note: 'Discipline-based classification of the work front zone indicating the primary trade or engineering discipline active at this location. Supports resource planning and subcontractor assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: civil|structural|mep|architectural|earthworks|piling|finishing|commissioning — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Master record for each active work front (zone, area, or face) on a construction site where concurrent work activities are executed. Tracks work front identity, location coordinates (GPS/grid reference), zone classification, active/inactive status, assigned foreman, mobilization date, demobilization date, current phase, associated WBS element, and parent site reference. Serves as the primary spatial and organizational anchor for all site-level transactional records — every operational product in this domain holds a mandatory or optional FK to work_front. The work_front-centric topology enables spatial aggregation of production, cost, progress, and resource data by zone.'
}

Table site.daily_log {
  daily_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the daily site log record. Primary key for the daily_log data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  contract_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_milestone. Business justification: Daily logs record the achievement of contract milestones and delay events affecting milestone dates. Superintendents link daily log entries to contract milestones for EOT substantiation and milestone ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Daily logs are aggregated by cost center for project cost performance reporting. Construction cost engineers produce daily cost center burn reports from site logs. No existing cost_center FK on daily_']
  drawing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.drawing. Business justification: Daily Log reports reference the specific design drawing used for the day\'s work; required for progress audit and compliance.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Daily delivery reconciliation process: site supervisors must confirm that GR postings in procurement match deliveries recorded in the daily log. This link enables three-way match verification and supp']
  primary_daily_construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the project this daily log belongs to. Links the site log to the master project record for earned value and cost control reporting.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.site. Business justification: A daily log is recorded for a specific construction site. daily_log currently has no FK to site.site despite being the official daily site log. Adding site_id establishes the direct parent-child relat']
  toolbox_meeting_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.toolbox_meeting. Business justification: Daily logs record whether a TBM was conducted (\'tbm_conducted_flag\') but have no FK to the actual toolbox_meeting record. Site supervisors and HSE managers need to verify TBM completion against daily ']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the daily log was formally approved by the project manager or designated reviewer. Marks the transition from submitted to approved status in the log lifecycle.']
  concrete_volume_m3 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total volume of concrete poured on site for the log date in cubic metres. Critical production metric for structural progress tracking, QA/QC compliance (ITP), and earned value computation. Sourced from Procore and HCSS HeavyJob.']
  cost_code varchar [note: 'HCSS HeavyJob cost code assigned to the primary work activity recorded in this daily log. Enables direct integration between field production data and job costing in Viewpoint Vista and SAP S/4HANA Project Systems.']
  cost_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any event recorded in this daily log has been flagged as having a potential cost impact requiring a Change Order (CO) or variation claim. True triggers cost impact assessment and contract administration workflow.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this daily log record was first created in the system. Audit trail timestamp for data lineage and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  direct_labour_count varchar [note: 'Number of direct (GC-employed) workers on site for the log date, excluding subcontractor personnel. Used for workforce planning, payroll reconciliation, and labour productivity benchmarking.']
  earthworks_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volume of earthworks (cut, fill, or excavation) completed on site for the log date in cubic metres. Key production quantity for earned value computation, BOQ progress measurement, and HCSS HeavyJob production tracking.']
  eot_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any event recorded in this daily log has been flagged as having a potential Extension of Time (EOT) impact on the project programme. True initiates EOT substantiation documentation under FIDIC Clause 8.4 or applicable contract conditions.']
  equipment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of major equipment units (plant and machinery) present and operational on site for the log date. Used for equipment utilisation tracking, cost coding in HCSS HeavyJob, and mobilisation/demobilisation records.']
  event_count varchar [note: 'Total number of embedded line-item events (delay events, weather stoppages, utility strikes, design holds, access restrictions, safety observations) recorded within this daily log header. Supports event density analytics and claim complexity assessment.']
  has_delay_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more delay events were recorded in the embedded line-item events for this daily log. True triggers downstream delay analysis workflows and EOT claim tracking. Key filter for delay analytics.']
  has_safety_observation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more HSE safety observations or incidents were recorded in the embedded line-item events for this daily log. True triggers integration with Intelex HSE management for incident reporting and corrective action workflows.']
  hcss_log_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this daily log to the corresponding field operations record in HCSS HeavyJob. Supports production tracking reconciliation and cost code validation between Procore and HCSS.']
  log_date date [note: 'The calendar date for which this daily site log records field activities. The principal business event date — one log per project site per calendar day. Drives schedule performance and delay analysis.']
  log_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known sequential log number assigned by the construction management system (Procore) for this daily log. Used as the human-readable business identifier in correspondence, delay claims, and EOT substantiation. Format: DL-{ProjectCode}-{Year}-{Sequence}.']
  log_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the daily log record. Draft indicates in-progress field entry; submitted indicates superintendent sign-off pending review; approved indicates accepted by project management; rejected indicates returned for correction; void indicates cancelled/superseded entry.']
  lti_occurred_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Lost Time Injury (LTI) occurred on site on this log date. True triggers mandatory incident reporting to Intelex HSE system, regulatory notification under OSHA, and TRIR (Total Recordable Incident Rate) calculation update.']
  ncr_raised_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Non-Conformance Report (NCR) was raised on this log date. True triggers integration with the QA/QC management workflow and requires corrective action documentation per ISO 9001.']
  overall_site_status varchar [note: 'High-level classification of site productivity for the log date. Productive indicates full operations; partially_productive indicates reduced output due to delays or restrictions; non_productive indicates no billable work performed; shutdown indicates planned or emergency site closure.']
  permit_to_work_count varchar [note: 'Number of active Permits to Work (PTW) issued and in force on site for the log date. Supports HSE compliance monitoring, Intelex PTW tracking, and regulatory audit evidence for high-risk work activities.']
  planned_activities_narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the construction activities that were planned for the log date per the project schedule. Compared against work_performed_narrative to identify schedule variances and support look-ahead planning.']
  precipitation_mm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total rainfall or precipitation recorded at the site in millimetres for the log date. Key metric for weather delay claims, earthworks productivity assessment, and EOT substantiation under FIDIC Clause 8.4.']
  procore_log_reference varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this daily log record in the Procore Construction Management platform. Enables traceability and reconciliation between the Silver Layer data product and the operational system of record.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional free-text remarks or notes entered by the superintendent for items not captured in structured fields or the work performed narrative. Provides supplementary context for project management review and legal record completeness.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Type of work shift recorded in this daily log. Distinguishes day, night, double-shift, or split-shift operations. Relevant for overtime cost tracking, HSE fatigue management, and productivity benchmarking.']
  site_access_status varchar [note: 'Operational access status of the construction site for the log date. Closed or restricted access directly impacts productivity and supports delay claim documentation. Partial indicates some work fronts accessible.']
  subcontractor_count varchar [note: 'Number of subcontractor personnel on site for the log date. Supports subcontractor performance monitoring, site coordination, and contract administration.']
  superintendent_sign_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the site superintendent formally signed off and submitted the daily log. Establishes the legal record timestamp for the site activity log. Critical for delay claim and EOT substantiation timelines.']
  tbm_conducted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Toolbox Meeting (TBM) was conducted on site for the log date. Mandatory HSE compliance indicator. True confirms daily safety briefing was held per SWMS (Safe Work Method Statement) requirements.']
  temperature_high_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Highest recorded ambient temperature at the site in degrees Celsius for the log date. Used for concrete pour suitability assessment, HSE heat stress monitoring, and weather delay substantiation.']
  temperature_low_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lowest recorded ambient temperature at the site in degrees Celsius for the log date. Used for cold weather concreting assessment, frost protection requirements, and weather delay substantiation.']
  total_delay_duration_hrs decimal(6,2) [note: 'Aggregate duration in hours of all delay events recorded in the embedded line-item events for this daily log. Used for schedule impact quantification, EOT claim substantiation, and SPI (Schedule Performance Index) computation.']
  total_manpower_count varchar [note: 'Total number of workers (all trades combined) present on site for the log date. Includes direct labour and subcontractor personnel. Key input for productivity analysis, earned value computation, and labour cost reconciliation against HCSS HeavyJob cost codes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this daily log record was last modified. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer and audit trail for record changes.']
  visitor_count varchar [note: 'Number of authorised visitors (client representatives, inspectors, auditors, regulatory officials) on site for the log date. Supports site access management, HSE induction compliance, and client engagement tracking.']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'Primary WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) code associated with the dominant work activity performed on this log date. Links site production data to the project schedule in Oracle Primavera P6 for earned value and progress reporting.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Observed general weather condition at the site for the log date. Used to substantiate weather-related delay claims, EOT applications, and force majeure events under FIDIC contract provisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|partly_cloudy|overcast|rain|heavy_rain|snow|fog|storm|extreme_heat — promote to reference product]']
  wind_speed_kmh decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recorded wind speed at the site in kilometres per hour for the log date. Used to assess crane and elevated work restrictions, HSE compliance, and weather-related work stoppages.']
  work_performed_narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text superintendent narrative describing all construction activities performed on site during the log date. Serves as the primary operational record and legal evidence base for progress claims, delay substantiation, and EOT applications. Sourced from Procore Daily Logs.']

  Note: 'Official daily site log (header + line-item structure) capturing all field activities for a given project site and date. The header records weather conditions (temperature, precipitation, wind), site access status, manpower headcount by trade, equipment on site, work performed narrative, superintendent sign-off, and overall site status. Embedded line-item events capture each notable occurrence including delay events, weather stoppages, utility strikes, design holds, access restrictions, and safety observations — each with event type, start/end time, duration, affected work fronts, root cause classification, responsible party, and EOT/cost impact flags. This is the SSOT for all site event data — no separate event entity exists. Sourced from Procore Daily Logs module. Serves as the legal and operational record of site activity and the primary evidence base for delay claims and EOT substantiation.'
}

Table site.production_entry {
  production_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each granular production tracking record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the production_entry data product.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project under which this production entry is recorded. Links production quantities to the project domain for earned value computation.']
  cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost code used to classify and track the financial dimension of this production entry. Sourced from HCSS HeavyJob cost coding module and aligned with SAP S/4HANA job costing.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the crew or gang assigned to execute the production work for this entry. Links to workforce domain for labor cost and productivity analysis.']
