  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: Client account ownership of a site is fundamental to billing, access control, and client-facing site reporting. Construction domain experts universally expect a site to be attributed to a commissionin']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project to which the site belongs.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Construction sites are managed as cost centers for overhead allocation and site establishment cost reporting. site has a plain cost_center_code column that is a denormalized reference to finance.cost_']
  current_location_site_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on site (current_location_site_id)']
  originating_client_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_opportunity. Business justification: Pipeline-to-execution traceability: a site is mobilised as a direct result of winning a client opportunity. BD and project controls teams require this link for win-rate analysis, opportunity-to-site c']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the site.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (e.g., suite or building).']
  area_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total built‑up area of the site in square feet.']
  category varchar [note: 'High‑level business category of the site.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the site is located.']
  code varchar [note: 'External business identifier or code assigned to the site.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the site location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the site record was first created in the system.']
  crew_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of crew members that can be assigned to the site simultaneously.']
  demobilization_date date [note: 'Date when the site was demobilized.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the site, including notes on purpose or special characteristics.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the site was closed or demobilized (nullable).']
  environmental_permit_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the environmental permit associated with the site.']
  gps_accuracy_m varchar [note: 'Estimated accuracy of the GPS coordinates in meters.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest inspection.']
  is_mobilized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site is currently mobilized for work.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety or compliance inspection.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the site location.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the site location.']
  mobilization_date date [note: 'Date when the site was mobilized.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the construction site.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Organization that owns the site.']
  permit_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the environmental permit.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the site address.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region where the site is located.']
  safety_incident_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of safety incidents recorded at the site.']
  site_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the site.']
  site_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the site based on its primary function.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the site became operational or was mobilized.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the site location.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the site record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for site. Referenced by current_location_site_id.'
}

Table design.transmittal {
  transmittal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the transmittal record. Primary key for the transmittal entity.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project for which this transmittal is issued. Links transmittal to the parent project context.']
  recipient_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Transmittal correspondence management requires linking each transmittal to the specific client contact who received it. Supports contractual correspondence audit trails, dispute resolution, and client']
  sender_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: Transmittal origin must be linked to the client account for contract document tracking and regulatory filing.']
  acknowledgement_by varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who provided formal acknowledgement on behalf of the recipient organization. Establishes personal accountability for receipt confirmation.']
  acknowledgement_date date [note: 'The date when the recipient formally acknowledged receipt of the transmittal. Null if not yet acknowledged. Provides legally defensible proof of document delivery.']
  acknowledgement_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator specifying whether the recipient is required to formally acknowledge receipt of the transmittal. True indicates acknowledgement is mandatory.']
  acknowledgement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of recipient acknowledgement tracking. Indicates whether the transmittal has been formally acknowledged by the recipient organization.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level indicating the sensitivity and access restrictions applicable to the transmittal and its enclosed documents.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transmittal record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record origination.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'The method or channel used to physically or electronically deliver the transmittal to the recipient. Tracks distribution logistics.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the transmittal contents, context, and any special instructions or notes for the recipient. Provides comprehensive background information.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'The primary engineering or design discipline to which the transmittal documents belong. Categorizes transmittals by technical specialization for routing and review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: architectural|structural|mechanical|electrical|plumbing|civil|geotechnical|environmental — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  document_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual documents or drawings included in this transmittal package. Used for completeness verification and audit trail.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which the recipient is expected to respond or take action on the transmittal, particularly relevant for approval or review purposes. Null if no response deadline applies.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date when the transmittal was formally issued and dispatched to the recipient organization. Represents the principal business event timestamp for document distribution.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transmittal record was last modified or updated. Tracks the most recent change to the record for audit and version control purposes.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the transmittal indicating the urgency of review or response required from the recipient. Guides workflow prioritization.']
  purpose_of_issue varchar [note: 'The intended purpose or action required from the recipient upon receiving the transmittal. Defines the business intent behind the document distribution and guides recipient response obligations.']
  recipient_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the recipient contact for delivery confirmation and acknowledgement of the transmittal.']
  recipient_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or company receiving the transmittal. Identifies the party to whom the documents are being dispatched.']
  reference_transmittal_number varchar [note: 'Transmittal number of a previous or related transmittal that this transmittal references, supersedes, or responds to. Establishes document lineage and traceability.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the transmittal that do not fit in other structured fields. Provides supplementary context.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision identifier for the transmittal itself if it has been reissued or updated. Tracks version history of the transmittal record.']
  sender_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual within the sender organization who prepared and issued the transmittal. Provides personal accountability for document distribution.']
  sender_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the sender contact for correspondence and queries related to the transmittal.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief descriptive title or subject line summarizing the content and purpose of the transmittal. Provides quick context for document package identification.']
  transmittal_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the transmittal as recognized by all project parties. Typically follows project-specific numbering conventions and serves as the externally-known reference for document distribution tracking.']
  transmittal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the transmittal indicating its position in the document distribution workflow. Tracks progression from draft through issuance to acknowledgement or closure.']

  Note: 'Records the formal dispatch of documents, drawings, and submittals between project parties via Aconex transmittals. Captures transmittal number, issue date, sender organization, recipient organization, purpose of issue (for approval, for information, for construction, for record), document list, and acknowledgement status. Provides a legally defensible audit trail of document distribution throughout the project lifecycle. Canonical design.transmittal entity (v2 curated).'
}

Table design.rfi {
  rfi_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for rfi']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: RFIs in construction are directed to or raised by a specific client contact (e.g., client\'s design manager). Linking RFI to the client contact enables RFI response SLA tracking, client reporting, and ']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project to which this RFI belongs.']
  finance_cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: RFIs may result in cost changes; linking each RFI to the relevant cost code enables cost impact analysis.']
  actual_response_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the RFI response was provided and recorded in the system.']
  closure_date date [note: 'The date on which the RFI was formally closed after response acceptance and any required follow-up actions were completed.']
  closure_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments recorded at the time of RFI closure, documenting final resolution or outstanding items.']
  cost_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact (in project currency) resulting from the RFI clarification, if applicable.']
  cost_impact_flag decimal(18,2) [note: 'Indicates whether the RFI response has identified a potential cost impact to the project, triggering change order evaluation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this RFI record was first created in the data platform.']
  date_raised date [note: 'The date on which the RFI was formally submitted or raised in the project management system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the question, issue, or clarification being requested, including context and specific areas of concern.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'The engineering or design discipline to which this RFI pertains (e.g., architectural, structural, MEP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: architectural|structural|mechanical|electrical|plumbing|civil|geotechnical — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this RFI in the source system, used for traceability and reconciliation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this RFI record was last updated in the data platform, supporting audit trail and change tracking.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the RFI indicating urgency and impact on project schedule or critical path.']
  response_content varchar [note: 'Detailed text of the response provided by the addressee, including clarifications, instructions, or references to supporting documentation.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'The contractual or agreed-upon date by which a response to the RFI is required, often tied to project schedule milestones.']
  response_time_days varchar [note: 'Calculated number of calendar days between date raised and actual response date, used for performance tracking and SLA compliance.']
  rfi_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the RFI, typically a sequential or structured code used for tracking and reference in project documentation and correspondence.']
  rfi_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RFI indicating its position in the review and response workflow.']
  schedule_impact_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of calendar days of schedule delay resulting from the RFI clarification, if applicable.']
  schedule_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the RFI response has identified a potential schedule impact, potentially triggering an Extension of Time (EOT) request.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief title or subject line summarizing the nature of the clarification request.']

  Note: 'Request for Information records capturing formal queries raised by contractors seeking clarification on design intent, specifications, or contract documents. Tracks RFI number, subject, discipline, originator, addressee, date raised, response due date, actual response date, response content, cost/schedule impact assessment, linked drawing or specification reference, and closure status. High-volume transactional entity critical for design coordination and contractual record-keeping. Sourced from Procore RFI module and Aconex. Canonical design.rfi entity (v2 curated).'
}

Table design.document_register {
  document_register_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the document register entry. Primary key for the document register product.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project this document belongs to.']
  contract_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subcontractor.contract_agreement. Business justification: Documents often belong to a subcontractor contract; FK supports contract‑based document control, retention, and regulatory reporting.']
  rfi_document_rfi_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.rfi. Business justification: RFI is currently siloed; adding a foreign key from document_register to rfi creates an inbound link and removes the redundant string reference column.']
  transmittal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.transmittal. Business justification: document_register.transmittal_number is a denormalized STRING reference to the most recent transmittal associated with this document. Replacing with transmittal_id FK normalizes the relationship and r']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the document received formal approval from the designated approver.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who provided final approval for the document to be issued.']
  confidentiality_classification varchar [note: 'Security classification level of the document content determining access rights and distribution restrictions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this document register record was first created in the system.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'Engineering or construction discipline responsible for the document. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: civil|structural|architectural|mechanical|electrical|plumbing|instrumentation|controls|process|geotechnical|environmental|HSE|quality|project_management — promote to reference product]']
  distribution_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of parties, roles, or organizations to whom this document should be distributed.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the document following project numbering convention. This is the externally-known document reference used across all project communications and systems.']
  document_purpose varchar [note: 'Brief statement describing the intended use and purpose of the document within the project context.']
  document_register_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the document in the approval and distribution workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_review|approved|issued_for_construction|issued_for_information|issued_for_tender|superseded|archived|void|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  document_title varchar [note: 'Full descriptive title of the document as it appears on the document itself.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the document by its primary purpose and content type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: specification|report|procedure|method_statement|manual|certificate|correspondence|transmittal|inspection_plan|quality_plan|test_report|safety_plan|environmental_plan|commissioning_plan|handover_document|as_built_record|warranty|permit — promote to reference product]']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Digital file format of the document (e.g., PDF for specifications, DWG for CAD drawings, RVT for Revit models). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PDF|DOCX|XLSX|DWG|RVT|IFC|XML — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_size_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Size of the document file in megabytes for storage and transmission planning.']
  is_client_deliverable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this document is a contractual deliverable to the client requiring formal acceptance.']
  is_controlled_document boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this document is subject to formal change control procedures and requires approval for any modifications.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date when the current revision of the document was formally issued or published.']
  keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of keywords or tags for document search and categorization purposes.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 639-2 code representing the primary language of the document content. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ENG|SPA|FRA|DEU|ARA|CHI|POR — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this document register record was last updated or modified.']
  originator varchar [note: 'Organization or party responsible for creating and issuing the document (e.g., design consultant, contractor, subcontractor, client).']
  page_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pages in the document for printing and review planning purposes.']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years the document must be retained per regulatory, contractual, or organizational requirements before eligible for disposal.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the document review must be completed to maintain project schedule.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who reviewed the document for technical accuracy and completeness.']
  revision_description varchar [note: 'Brief description of the changes made in the current revision compared to the previous version.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Current revision identifier of the document following project revision convention (e.g., A, B, C or 01, 02, 03 or R0, R1, R2).']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical or digital location where the document is stored (e.g., Aconex folder path, SharePoint site, physical archive location).']
  superseded_by_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the newer document that supersedes this document, if applicable. Used to maintain document lineage and version control.']
  supersedes_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the older document that this document supersedes or replaces.']

  Note: 'Central master register of all project documents beyond drawings — including specifications, reports, procedures, method statements, O&M manuals, certificates, and correspondence — managed in Aconex CDE (Common Data Environment). Captures document number, title, document type, discipline, revision, status, originator, issue date, confidentiality classification, retention period, and numbering convention. Provides the authoritative catalog of all project documentation per ISO 19650 information container requirements. Canonical design.document_register entity (v2 curated).'
}

Table design.revision {
  revision_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for revision']
  document_register_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent document in the document register. Links this revision to its master document record.']
  superseded_revision_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous revision that this version replaces. Maintains the revision chain and version history.']
  transmittal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.transmittal. Business justification: revision.transmittal_number is a denormalized STRING reference to the transmittal that distributed this document revision. Replacing it with a proper FK transmittal_id → design.transmittal.transmittal']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this revision was officially approved for issuance. Represents a distinct lifecycle event in the approval workflow.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who approved this revision for release. Authorized signatory for document issuance.']
  author_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who authored or prepared this revision. Responsible party for the content creation.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason or trigger for this revision. Categorizes the business driver behind the document update.']
  change_summary varchar [note: 'Concise summary of the specific changes made in this revision compared to the previous version. Highlights key modifications, additions, or deletions.']
  checksum_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash (e.g., SHA-256) of the file content. Ensures file integrity and detects unauthorized modifications.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes, remarks, or instructions related to this revision. Captures supplementary information not covered by other fields.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this revision record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the purpose and scope of this revision. Explains what changed and why.']
  distribution_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of recipients or distribution groups who should receive this revision. Tracks who needs to be notified of the update.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'File type or format of the document (e.g., PDF, DWG for CAD, RVT for BIM). Indicates the software required to open the file. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pdf|dwg|dxf|rvt|ifc|docx|xlsx|pptx — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_name varchar [note: 'Name of the physical file associated with this revision, including extension (e.g., Drawing_A101_RevB.pdf).']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the document file in bytes. Used for storage management and download estimation.']
  file_storage_path varchar [note: 'Full path or URI to the physical file location in the document management system or cloud storage. Enables retrieval of the actual document file.']
  is_controlled_copy boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this revision is a controlled copy subject to formal change management. True for official project copies, false for reference or uncontrolled copies.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this revision record was last updated in the system. Audit trail for record modifications.']
  page_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pages in this revision. Used for printing, archiving, and completeness verification.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the review of this revision was completed. Represents a distinct lifecycle event in the approval workflow.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who reviewed this revision for technical accuracy and completeness.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date when this revision was officially issued or published. Represents the business event timestamp for the revision release.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential or alphanumeric identifier for this revision (e.g., Rev A, Rev 01, R1.2). Represents the version sequence within the document lifecycle.']
  revision_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this revision. Tracks the approval and publication workflow state.']
  revision_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the revision based on the nature and impact of changes. Distinguishes between routine updates and significant modifications.']
  sheet_count varchar [note: 'Total number of sheets or drawings in this revision (applicable for CAD and BIM documents). Used for drawing set completeness checks.']

  Note: 'Version control record for each document in the document register, capturing revision identifier, revision date, revision description, author, reviewer, approver, change summary, superseded revision reference, and file storage path. Maintains a complete and auditable version history for all project documents to support regulatory compliance, handover, and DLP (Defects Liability Period) obligations. Canonical design.revision entity (v2 curated).'
}

Table design.workflow_approval {
  workflow_approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the workflow approval instance. Primary key for the workflow approval entity.']
  client_reviewer_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Client contacts frequently participate as external reviewers in document approval workflows (e.g., client\'s engineer approving IFC drawings). Linking to client.contact supports approval audit trails, ']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project to which this workflow approval belongs. Enables project-level reporting and audit trails.']
  document_register_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the document or deliverable under review in this workflow. Links to the document management system record.']
  revision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.revision. Business justification: workflow_approval.revision_number is a denormalized STRING reference to the document revision under review. In document control, approval workflows are initiated for a specific revision of a document ']
  submittal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.design_submittal. Business justification: In construction design management, formal multi-step approval workflows (tracked in workflow_approval) are frequently initiated specifically for design submittals — shop drawings, material submittals,']
  action_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reviewer recorded their approval decision or action. Used for SLA compliance tracking and audit trail.']
  action_taken varchar [note: 'The decision or action recorded by the reviewer at the current workflow step. Determines routing to next step or workflow termination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: approved|approved_with_comments|rejected|no_objection|commented|delegated|returned_for_revision|acknowledged|pending — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approval_authority_level varchar [note: 'The organizational or contractual authority level required to approve this workflow. Determines who has final sign-off rights per delegation of authority matrix. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: project_team|discipline_lead|project_manager|design_manager|technical_director|client_representative|regulatory_authority — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assigned_reviewer_role varchar [note: 'The functional role or position assigned to review the current step (e.g., Lead Engineer, Design Manager, QA/QC Manager, Project Director). Defines approval authority.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this workflow approval to the complete audit trail log in the document management system. Supports ISO 9001 and ISO 19650 compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this workflow approval record was first created in the data platform. Audit field for data lineage and record lifecycle tracking.']
  current_step_sequence varchar [note: 'The ordinal position of the current approval step in the workflow chain. Used to track progress and determine next routing action.']
  delegation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the original reviewer for delegating the approval to another individual (e.g., absence, conflict of interest, technical expertise).']
  due_date date [note: 'Target completion date for the entire workflow approval process. Derived from project schedule, contractual milestones, or regulatory deadlines.']
  escalation_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the workflow was escalated to higher approval authority or management attention due to delay or complexity.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this workflow step was escalated to higher authority due to SLA breach, complexity, or policy requirements. True if escalated.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this workflow approval in the source system (Aconex workflow ID, BIM 360 review ID). Enables bi-directional traceability.']
  initiated_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the workflow approval was initiated and entered the approval chain. Marks the start of the approval lifecycle and SLA clock.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this workflow approval record was last updated in the data platform. Audit field for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  last_reminder_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the most recent reminder notification was sent to the assigned reviewer. Used to schedule next reminder per escalation policy.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether automated notification was sent to the assigned reviewer when the workflow step was routed to them. True if notification sent.']
  outcome_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the final workflow outcome was determined and the approval process was closed. Marks the end of the approval lifecycle.']
  overall_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the complete workflow approval process after all steps are completed. Determines document status and next business actions.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the workflow approval. Influences SLA targets, routing urgency, and reviewer notification frequency.']
  regulatory_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this workflow approval is required to satisfy regulatory, statutory, or building code compliance obligations. True if regulatory-driven.']
  reminder_count varchar [note: 'Number of automated reminder notifications sent to the assigned reviewer for pending action. Used to track follow-up frequency and SLA risk.']
  reviewer_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments, observations, or conditions recorded by the reviewer. Captures technical feedback, approval conditions, or reasons for rejection.']
  sla_actual_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The actual elapsed time in hours from workflow step assignment to action completion. Used to calculate SLA compliance and identify bottlenecks.']
  sla_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the workflow step was completed within the SLA target duration. True if compliant, False if breached.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'The contractual or policy-defined target duration in hours for completing this workflow step. Used to measure compliance with approval turnaround commitments.']
  total_steps varchar [note: 'Total number of approval steps defined in the workflow template. Used to calculate completion percentage and remaining steps.']
  workflow_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the workflow approval instance, typically derived from the document title or approval purpose.']
  workflow_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the workflow approval instance. Externally visible reference number used in correspondence and audit trails.']
  workflow_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the workflow approval. Tracks progression through the approval chain from initiation to final outcome. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initiated|in_progress|pending_review|approved|rejected|on_hold|cancelled|completed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  workflow_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the workflow approval by business purpose. Determines routing rules, SLA targets, and approval authority requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: design_review|submittal_approval|rfi_response|change_order_approval|drawing_approval|specification_review|technical_query|document_transmittal|noncompliance_review|method_statement_approval — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Formal document review and approval workflow instances tracking multi-step approval chains in Aconex and BIM 360 CDE. Captures workflow template, document under review, step sequence, assigned reviewer (role and individual), action taken (approved, rejected, commented, delegated, no objection), action date, reviewer comments, SLA target vs actual duration, and overall workflow outcome. Provides the legally required audit trail of all document approval decisions for ISO 9001 quality management, ISO 19650 information management, and contractual compliance under FIDIC/NEC. Canonical design.workflow_approval entity (v2 curated).'
}

Table design.bim_model {
  bim_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the BIM model record. Primary key for the BIM model entity.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project this BIM model belongs to. Links the model to its parent project context.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to material.material_master. Business justification: BIM model material library requires linking each model to its material master for quantity take‑off and clash detection.']
  superseded_by_model_bim_model_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer BIM model version that replaces this one. Null if this is the current version.']
  author_organization varchar [note: 'Organization or company responsible for authoring the BIM model (e.g., design consultant, contractor).']
  authoring_software varchar [note: 'Software application used to create and edit the BIM model (e.g., Autodesk Revit, Bentley MicroStation, ArchiCAD, Tekla Structures).']
  building_zone varchar [note: 'Specific building, zone, or facility area that this BIM model represents (e.g., Tower A, Basement Level 2, North Wing).']
  clash_count varchar [note: 'Number of unresolved clashes identified in the latest clash detection run. Zero indicates coordination clearance.']
  clash_detection_status varchar [note: 'Status of clash detection analysis for this model against other discipline models. Critical for coordination.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes, remarks, or special instructions related to the BIM model version.']
  confidentiality_classification varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the BIM model determining access controls and distribution restrictions.']
  coordinate_system varchar [note: 'Coordinate reference system used for model positioning (e.g., WGS84, NAD83, local grid). Ensures spatial alignment across disciplines.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this BIM model record was first created in the system.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'Engineering or design discipline that owns this BIM model. Determines the technical domain and responsible team. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: architectural|structural|mechanical|electrical|plumbing|civil|geotechnical|landscape|interior_design|fire_protection|telecommunications|environmental — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  element_count varchar [note: 'Total number of BIM elements (objects) contained in the model. Indicator of model complexity.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier from the source system (Autodesk BIM 360, Bentley ProjectWise) for integration and synchronization.']
  federation_role decimal(9,4) [note: 'Role of this model in federated model assembly. Determines how it integrates with other discipline models.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Native or exchange file format of the BIM model. Determines interoperability and viewing requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RVT|NWD|NWC|IFC|DWG|DGN|SKP|RFA|DXF — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_size_mb decimal(12,2) [note: 'Size of the BIM model file in megabytes. Used for storage planning and performance optimization.']
  iso_19650_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the BIM model meets ISO 19650 information management requirements and standards.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date when this version of the BIM model was formally issued or published to the project team.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this BIM model record was last updated in the system.']
  lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Stage of the project lifecycle this model version represents. Aligns with ISO 19650 delivery phases. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concept|design_development|construction_documentation|construction|commissioning|operations|decommissioning — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lod_classification varchar [note: 'Level of Development per AIA E202 and BIMForum LOD Specification. Defines the degree of geometric detail and information richness in the model elements.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the BIM model describing its content and scope (e.g., Terminal Building Level 1 Architecture, Bridge Deck Structural Model).']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the BIM model, typically following project naming conventions (e.g., ARC-L01-001, STR-F02-005). Used for external reference and communication.']
  model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the BIM model. Controls access, usage, and workflow progression.']
  model_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the model purpose and usage context within the project lifecycle.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this iteration of the BIM model. Tracks evolution and change history.']
  origin_elevation_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Elevation of the model origin point in meters above sea level. Provides vertical datum reference.']
  origin_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the model origin point in decimal degrees. Establishes geolocation reference.']
  origin_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the model origin point in decimal degrees. Establishes geolocation reference.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the BIM model review was completed and approved for issue.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who performed technical review of the BIM model before publication.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Formal revision number following project document control conventions (e.g., P01, C02, A03).']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the authoring software used to create the model. Critical for compatibility and file exchange.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'File path or cloud storage location where the BIM model file is stored (e.g., BIM 360 project folder, SharePoint path).']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'Work Breakdown Structure code linking the BIM model to specific project scope and cost accounts.']

  Note: 'Master record for each Building Information Model (BIM) asset managed in Autodesk BIM 360, Bentley ProjectWise, or equivalent platform. Captures model identity, discipline (architectural, structural, MEP, civil), authoring software and version, file format (RVT, IFC, NWD), project coordinate system, geolocation reference point, LOD (Level of Development) classification per AIA E202/BIMForum LOD Specification, model status (WIP, shared, published, archived), lifecycle stage, federation role (host/linked), and file size. SSOT for all 3D model metadata across the project portfolio, enabling federated model assembly, clash detection workflows, 4D/5D BIM integration, and ISO 19650 information management compliance. Canonical design.bim_model entity (v2 curated).'
}

Table design.drawing {
  drawing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the engineering or construction drawing record. Primary key.']
  bim_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.bim_model. Business justification: Drawing belongs to a BIM model; replace string reference with proper FK to BIM model for traceability.']
  client_approver_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Drawings in construction require formal client sign-off before construction proceeds. Linking to the specific client contact who approved the drawing supports contractual drawing approval audit trails']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project to which this drawing belongs.']
  cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Construction drawings are assigned cost codes for design phase cost tracking and earned value measurement. Cost engineers allocate drawing production costs to specific cost codes; this link enables de']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to material.material_master. Business justification: Drawings contain material call‑outs; linking to material_master enables procurement and verification of specified materials.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the drawing was formally approved for issue.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who approved the drawing for issue.']
  cad_file_name varchar [note: 'Name of the CAD source file from which the drawing was generated.']
  checker_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who performed technical checking or peer review of the drawing.']
  clash_detection_status varchar [note: 'Status of BIM clash detection analysis for this drawing (e.g., not checked, passed, clashes detected, resolved).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, remarks, or context about the drawing.']
  confidentiality_classification varchar [note: 'Data classification level indicating the sensitivity and access restrictions for the drawing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the drawing record was first created in the system.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'Engineering discipline or trade responsible for the drawing (e.g., architectural, structural, MEP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: architectural|structural|mechanical|electrical|plumbing|civil|geotechnical|environmental — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  distribution_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of roles, organizations, or individuals to whom the drawing was distributed.']
  drawing_number varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to the drawing per project drawing numbering convention. Serves as the business key for the drawing register.']
  drawing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the drawing (e.g., draft, issued for review, issued for construction, approved, superseded, archived). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued_for_review|issued_for_approval|issued_for_construction|approved|superseded|archived — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  drawing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the drawing by its representation type (e.g., plan, elevation, section, detail, schedule, isometric).']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Digital file format of the drawing (e.g., PDF, DWG, DXF, RVT, IFC, DGN).']
  file_size_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Size of the drawing file in megabytes.']
  is_client_deliverable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the drawing is a contractual deliverable to the client.']
  is_controlled_document boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the drawing is subject to formal document control procedures.']
  issue_purpose varchar [note: 'Purpose for which the drawing was issued (e.g., for information, for review, for approval, for construction, for tender, as-built).']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code indicating the language in which the drawing annotations are written.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified the drawing record in the system.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the drawing record was last modified in the system.']
  originator varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or firm that created the drawing (e.g., design consultant, engineering firm).']
  page_count varchar [note: 'Number of pages or sheets in the drawing document.']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years the drawing must be retained per contractual, regulatory, or organizational policy.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date when the current revision of the drawing was issued.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Current revision identifier of the drawing (e.g., A, B, C, 01, 02). Tracks version history.']
  scale varchar [note: 'Scale ratio of the drawing (e.g., 1:100, 1:50, NTS for not to scale).']
  sheet_size varchar [note: 'Standard paper or sheet size of the drawing (e.g., A0, A1, ARCH D, ARCH E). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: A0|A1|A2|A3|A4|ARCH_D|ARCH_E — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'File path or URI indicating where the drawing file is stored in the document management system.']
  superseded_by_drawing_number varchar [note: 'Drawing number of the newer version that supersedes this drawing.']
  supersedes_drawing_number varchar [note: 'Drawing number of the previous version that this drawing supersedes or replaces.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the drawing indicating the scope, location, or system depicted.']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'Work Breakdown Structure code linking the drawing to a specific project work package or deliverable.']
  zone_location varchar [note: 'Physical zone, building, or location within the project site that the drawing depicts.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created the drawing record in the system.']

  Note: 'Master record for each engineering and construction drawing (CAD/BIM-derived) managed across the project. Tracks drawing number, title, discipline, sheet size, scale, revision number, current status (issued for construction, issued for review, superseded), originator, and approval state. Serves as the SSOT for the drawing register aligned with Autodesk BIM 360 document control. Canonical design.drawing entity (v2 curated).'
}

Table design.drawing_revision {
  drawing_revision_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each drawing revision event. Primary key for the drawing revision record.']
  bim_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.bim_model. Business justification: drawing_revision.bim_model_reference is a denormalized STRING reference to the BIM model from which this drawing revision was derived. In BIM-enabled construction projects, drawings are extracted from']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project this drawing revision is associated with. Enables project-level filtering and reporting.']
  drawing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent drawing document that this revision belongs to. Links to the master drawing record in the document management system.']
  rfi_document_rfi_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.rfi. Business justification: Provides a second inbound link to RFI from drawing_revision, consolidating the existing string reference into a proper FK.']
  superseded_revision_drawing_revision_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous revision that this revision replaces or supersedes. Maintains the version control chain and audit trail.']
  transmittal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.transmittal. Business justification: drawing_revision.transmittal_number is a denormalized STRING reference to the transmittal used to issue this drawing revision to project parties. Replacing with transmittal_id FK normalizes the relati']
  acknowledgment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether recipients are required to formally acknowledge receipt and review of this revision. Used for critical or contractual revisions.']

  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this revision received formal approval for issuance. Marks the transition from draft to approved status.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who approved this revision. Provides human-readable identification for the approval authority.']
  change_summary varchar [note: 'Brief summary of the key changes introduced in this revision. Provides quick reference for stakeholders reviewing revision history.']
  clash_detection_status varchar [note: 'Status of clash detection analysis for this revision. Indicates whether the design has been checked for spatial conflicts with other disciplines and the outcome.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes, remarks, or contextual information about this revision. Provides space for supplementary details not captured in structured fields.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Security classification level of this drawing revision. Controls access permissions and distribution restrictions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this revision record was first created in the data management system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'The engineering or design discipline responsible for this drawing revision. Enables discipline-based filtering and workflow routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: architectural|structural|mechanical|electrical|plumbing|civil|geotechnical|environmental|surveying|landscape — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  distribution_date date [note: 'Date when this revision was distributed to project stakeholders. Marks the formal communication of the revision to the project team.']
  distribution_method varchar [note: 'The method or channel used to distribute this revision to stakeholders. Tracks whether distribution was electronic, physical, or both.']
  distribution_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the distribution process for this revision. Tracks whether the revision has been sent to stakeholders and their acknowledgment status.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'The digital file format of this revision (e.g., PDF for distribution, DWG for CAD native, IFC for BIM exchange). Supports format-based filtering and compatibility checks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PDF|DWG|DXF|IFC|RVT|DGN|TIFF — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_size_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Size of the revision file in megabytes. Used for storage management and transmission planning.']
  issuing_engineer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the engineer who prepared and issued this revision. Provides human-readable identification for audit and communication purposes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this revision record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to the record for audit and synchronization purposes.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the technical review of this revision was completed. Tracks the review milestone in the approval workflow.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who reviewed this revision. Provides human-readable identification for the review step in the approval workflow.']
  revision_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the specific revision version (e.g., A, B, C, P01, P02 for preliminary, C01 for construction issue). Follows project-specific or organizational revision coding standards.']
  revision_date date [note: 'The date when this revision was officially issued or released. Represents the business event timestamp for the revision lifecycle.']
  revision_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the specific changes, modifications, or updates made in this revision. Provides context for what was altered from the previous version.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential numeric identifier for the revision within the drawing lifecycle. Increments with each new revision to maintain chronological order.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the business driver or trigger that necessitated this revision. Enables analysis of revision patterns and root causes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rfi_response|design_change|client_instruction|coordination_issue|code_compliance|value_engineering|constructability_review|error_correction|scope_change|material_substitution|site_condition|regulatory_requirement — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  revision_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the revision in its approval and distribution workflow. Tracks progression from draft through review, approval, issuance, and eventual supersession. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_review|approved|issued|superseded|void|archived — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  revision_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the intended purpose or stage of the revision within the project lifecycle. Distinguishes between preliminary design, construction issue, as-built documentation, etc. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: preliminary|for_approval|for_construction|for_tender|as_built|record|coordination — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sheet_count varchar [note: 'Number of sheets or pages in this drawing revision. Relevant for multi-sheet drawings and printing logistics.']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'The WBS element or work package code that this drawing revision is associated with. Enables project structure-based reporting and cost allocation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each revision event for a drawing. Stores revision code, revision date, description of changes, reason for revision (RFI-driven, design change, client instruction), issuing engineer, reviewer, approver, and distribution status. Enables full version-control audit trail for IFC/CAD drawing lifecycle. Canonical design.drawing_revision entity (v2 curated).'
}

Procurement, maintenance strategy, and regulatory compliance processes ']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project this technical specification belongs to. Links specification to project scope and WBS (Work Breakdown Structure).']
  cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Technical specifications are coded to cost codes for design cost allocation and BOQ cost estimation. In construction, specifications drive material and workmanship cost codes; this link enables specif']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to material.material_master. Business justification: Technical specifications list required materials; FK to material_master supports compliance checks and sourcing.']
  applicable_standards varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of industry standards, codes, and regulations that govern this specification (e.g., ACI 318, AISC 360, NFPA 70, ASTM C150, BS EN 1992).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the specification received formal approval from the designated approval authority (client, design manager, or regulatory body).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Formal approval state indicating whether the specification has received necessary sign-offs from design authority, client, or regulatory bodies.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual with authority who formally approved this specification for issue and use in construction.']
  approver_role varchar [note: 'Role or position of the approver (e.g., Lead Structural Engineer, Design Manager, Client Representative, QA/QC Manager).']
  author_name varchar [note: 'Name of the engineer or technical specialist who authored or prepared this specification document.']
  author_organization varchar [note: 'Organization or company responsible for authoring the specification (e.g., design consultant, engineering firm, EPC contractor).']
  boq_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to BOQ line items or sections that are governed by this technical specification, linking design requirements to cost estimation and procurement.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes, clarifications, or context regarding this specification, including change reasons, special instructions, or coordination notes.']
  confidentiality_classification varchar [note: 'Data classification level indicating the sensitivity and access restrictions for this specification document.']

  discipline varchar [note: 'Engineering discipline or trade category to which this specification applies. Used for organizing specifications by technical domain within MEP (Mechanical Electrical and Plumbing) and other construction disciplines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: civil|structural|architectural|mechanical|electrical|plumbing|hvac|instrumentation|process|geotechnical|environmental — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this specification becomes binding and applicable to construction activities, procurement, and quality control.']

  file_format varchar [note: 'Digital file format of the specification document (e.g., PDF, DOCX, native CAD format). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pdf|docx|doc|xlsx|dwg|ifc|native — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_storage_path varchar [note: 'Network path, document management system location, or cloud storage URI where the specification document file is stored (e.g., Aconex document ID, BIM 360 path).']
  hse_requirements varchar [note: 'Health, safety, and environmental requirements specific to this specification including PPE (Personal Protective Equipment), hazard controls, and OSHA compliance.']
  is_client_deliverable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this specification is a contractual deliverable to the client or owner, requiring formal submission and acceptance.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date when the current revision of the technical specification was formally issued or published to the project team.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code indicating the language in which the specification is written (e.g., EN for English, FR for French, AR for Arabic).']
