  invoiced_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The cumulative quantity that has been invoiced to date against this line item. Used for three-way matching and payment reconciliation.']
  item_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the line item type indicating the procurement scenario: standard purchase, service procurement, consignment, subcontracting, or stock transfer.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this purchase order line item record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line item number within the purchase order, used for ordering and referencing specific line items in the PO document.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the purchase order line item indicating its fulfillment state: open (not yet received), partially received, fully received, closed (completed), or cancelled.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'The original equipment manufacturer (OEM) part number for the material, used for quality assurance and warranty tracking.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the material or service being procured on this line, including specifications, grade, and any special requirements.']
  material_group varchar [note: 'Classification code grouping similar materials together for procurement analysis, vendor management, and spend reporting.']
  net_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total net value of this line item calculated as ordered quantity multiplied by unit price, before taxes and discounts.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material or service ordered on this line item, expressed in the unit of measure specified.']
  outstanding_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The remaining quantity yet to be delivered, calculated as ordered quantity minus goods receipt quantity. Critical for expediting and delivery monitoring.']
  over_delivery_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The acceptable percentage by which the vendor may over-deliver beyond the ordered quantity without requiring approval. Used for goods receipt tolerance checking.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The plant or site location code where the material will be delivered or the service will be performed. Used for multi-site inventory and logistics management.']
  price_unit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The quantity of units to which the unit price applies (e.g., price per 1, per 10, per 100 units). Used for bulk pricing scenarios.']
  requisitioner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person or department who requested this material or service, used for accountability and follow-up communication.']
  short_text varchar [note: 'Brief descriptive text for the line item, typically used for quick identification and reporting purposes.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'The specific storage location or warehouse within the plant where the material will be received and stored.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The calculated tax amount applicable to this line item based on the tax code and net value.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code that determines the applicable tax rate and tax jurisdiction for this line item.']
  under_delivery_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The acceptable percentage by which the vendor may under-deliver below the ordered quantity without penalty. Used for goods receipt tolerance checking.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the ordered quantity is measured (e.g., each, meter, square meter, cubic meter, kilogram, ton, liter, hour, day, set, lot, box, roll, bag). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|M|M2|M3|KG|TON|L|HR|DAY|SET|LOT|BOX|ROLL|BAG — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The price per unit of measure for the material or service on this line item, excluding taxes and additional charges.']
  vendor_material_number varchar [note: 'The suppliers own material or part number for the item being procured. Used for cross-referencing and supplier communication.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'The WBS element code to which this line item cost is assigned, enabling project-level cost tracking and allocation per CPM methodology.']

  Note: 'Individual line items within a purchase order capturing material or service description, ordered quantity, unit of measure, unit price, line total, delivery schedule date, WBS element, cost code, account assignment category, goods receipt indicator, invoice receipt indicator, and cumulative goods receipt quantity to date. Enables granular cost tracking and delivery monitoring at the PO line level.'
}

Table procurement.goods_receipt {
  goods_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for goods_receipt']
  batch_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to material.batch_lot. Business justification: Goods receipt in construction triggers batch/lot assignment for material traceability. Quality inspection, shelf-life tracking, and regulatory compliance all require linking the GR document to the spe']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project for which the materials were received. Enables project-level cost tracking and material allocation.']
  material_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.material_catalog. Business justification: Link goods receipt material to master catalog for normalization and remove redundant material_id column.']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line. Business justification: A goods receipt is physically received against a specific PO line item. The existing po_line_item_number (INT) is a denormalized line number reference that becomes redundant once a proper FK to po_lin']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order against which this goods receipt is recorded. Links the physical delivery to the procurement contract.']
  receiving_craft_worker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.craft_worker. Business justification: Goods receipts in construction require a named craft worker (foreman or inspector) to sign off on material delivery. This link supports quality accountability, regulatory audit trails, and three-way m']
  receiving_crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: The crew physically receiving and unloading materials on a construction site is a key operational link for site logistics, material accountability, and cost allocation to the correct crew. This suppor']
  technical_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.technical_specification. Business justification: Goods receipt QC inspection: Receiving inspectors verify delivered materials against the governing technical specification. This link enables spec-based acceptance criteria at goods receipt, supports ']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor who delivered the materials. Used for supplier performance tracking and three-way match verification.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer batch or lot number for the received materials. Critical for traceability, quality control, and recall management.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the transportation carrier or logistics provider who delivered the materials. Used for carrier performance tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the goods receipt value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for multi-currency financial reporting.']
  delivery_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this goods receipt completes the delivery for the purchase order line item (True=complete, False=partial delivery expected).']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'External delivery note or packing slip number provided by the vendor. Used for cross-referencing vendor shipment documentation.']
  gr_document_number varchar [note: 'Unique business document number assigned to this goods receipt transaction. Used for audit trail and cross-system reconciliation.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Quality inspection status for the received materials. Determines whether materials can be released to inventory or must remain in quarantine.']
  invoice_verification_status decimal(18,2) [note: 'Status of three-way match process comparing PO, goods receipt, and vendor invoice. Determines whether invoice can be paid.']
  material_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP material document number generated upon goods receipt posting. Used for inventory movement tracking and financial integration.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP movement type code indicating the type of inventory transaction (e.g., 101=GR for PO, 122=Return Delivery). Determines financial and inventory impact.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material originally ordered on the purchase order line item. Used for variance analysis against received quantity.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Financial posting date for the goods receipt transaction. Used for period-end closing and financial reporting alignment.']
  receipt_condition varchar [note: 'Condition status of the received materials upon inspection (accepted=fully accepted, rejected=fully rejected, partial=partially accepted, damaged=damaged on arrival, on_hold=pending inspection, quarantine=quality hold).']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which the materials were physically received at the site or warehouse. Used for lead time analysis and schedule compliance tracking.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the goods receipt was recorded in the system. Used for audit trail and real-time inventory updates.']
  received_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of material physically received and accepted. This is the quantity that updates inventory and triggers invoice verification.']
  rejected_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material rejected due to quality issues, damage, or non-conformance. Triggers vendor return or credit memo process.']
  reversal_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the reversal transaction if this goods receipt was cancelled. Used for audit trail and reconciliation.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this goods receipt has been reversed or cancelled (True=reversed, False=active). Reversed receipts do not update inventory.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description explaining why the goods receipt was reversed (e.g., incorrect posting, return to vendor, data entry error).']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number for serialized equipment or high-value items. Enables individual asset tracking and warranty management.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Any special handling requirements or instructions for the received materials (e.g., temperature control, hazardous material protocols, fragile handling).']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific storage location, warehouse bin, or laydown area where the materials are stored after receipt.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction code applied to the goods receipt for sales tax, VAT, or GST calculation. Determines tax treatment in financial posting.']
  total_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the goods receipt (received quantity × unit price). Updates inventory value and triggers accounts payable accrual.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Shipment tracking number provided by the carrier. Enables real-time delivery status monitoring and proof of delivery.']
  transportation_mode varchar [note: 'Method of transportation used to deliver the materials (truck, rail, air, sea, courier, pickup). Used for logistics analysis and lead time optimization.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the received quantity (e.g., EA=Each, M=Meter, M2=Square Meter, M3=Cubic Meter, KG=Kilogram, TON=Ton, L=Liter). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|M|M2|M3|KG|TON|L|BOX|PALLET|SET — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per unit of measure for the received material as per the purchase order. Used for inventory valuation and three-way match with invoice.']
  unloading_point varchar [note: 'Specific unloading dock, gate, or receiving area where the materials were delivered at the construction site.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Material valuation category used for inventory accounting (e.g., standard cost, moving average price). Determines how the receipt is valued financially.']
  variance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes explaining any discrepancies, damages, or special conditions observed during goods receipt. Used for dispute resolution and vendor performance tracking.']
  variance_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for any variance between ordered and received quantities (e.g., short shipment, over delivery, damaged goods).']

  Note: 'Material goods receipt records capturing physical delivery of materials or equipment to site or warehouse against a PO. Records GR document number, delivery date, received quantity, unit of measure, storage location, batch number, delivery note reference, condition on receipt (accepted, rejected, partial), receiving inspector, and SAP material document number. Triggers inventory update and three-way match for invoice verification.'
}

Table procurement.purchase_requisition {
  purchase_requisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the purchase requisition. Primary key for the purchase requisition entity.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project for which this purchase requisition is raised. Links the requisition to the project master data for cost tracking and budget control.']
  cost_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.cost_account. Business justification: Budget availability checking at PR approval requires linking requisitions to project cost accounts. Construction PMO processes validate PR cost against cost account budgets before approval. No existin']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: PRs are raised by cost centers in construction organizations. Proper FK replaces denormalized cost_center_code text field, enabling cost center budget availability checks, overhead allocation reportin']
  cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Purchase requisitions in construction are coded to cost codes for budget allocation and cost tracking. Proper FK to finance.cost_code enables cost code validation at requisition stage, supports cost r']
  drawing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.drawing. Business justification: Material Take-Off (MTO) process: PRs in construction are raised against specific drawings that define what materials are needed. The existing \'mto_reference\' plain-text field denormalizes this relatio']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to material.material_master. Business justification: Purchase requisitions are created for specific materials; linking to material master enables accurate budgeting, inventory checks, and compliance with approved material lists.']
  preferred_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: purchase_requisition has a preferred_vendor_code (STRING) — a denormalized vendor reference used by requisitioners to suggest a preferred supplier. Replacing this with a proper FK preferred_vendor_id ']
  project_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.project_budget. Business justification: Construction PRs require budget availability validation before approval. Linking PR to project_budget enables real-time budget commitment tracking, supports the budget_available_flag and budget_varian']
  scope_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_scope. Business justification: Purchase requisitions in construction are raised against specific contract scope items (work packages). This link enables scope-level procurement budget tracking and ensures PRs are authorized within ']
  technical_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.technical_specification. Business justification: Spec-driven requisitioning: PRs must reference the technical specification defining material quality and type requirements. The boolean \'technical_specification_attached\' flag is a weak substitute for']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase requisition received final approval and became eligible for conversion to RFQ or PO.']
  budget_available_flag decimal(18,2) [note: 'Indicates whether sufficient budget is available in the assigned WBS element or cost center to cover the estimated cost of this requisition. True if budget is available, False if insufficient.']
  budget_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between available budget and the estimated total cost. Positive value indicates surplus budget, negative indicates deficit requiring approval override.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase requisition was administratively closed after full conversion to procurement documents or cancellation, marking the end of its active lifecycle.']
  conversion_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase requisition was converted to an RFQ or PO document.']
  conversion_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether and how the approved purchase requisition has been converted into downstream procurement documents (RFQ for competitive bidding or direct PO for known vendors).']
  converted_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the RFQ or PO that was created from this purchase requisition, establishing traceability through the procurement lifecycle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the purchase requisition record was first created in the ERP system. Used for audit trail and process cycle time analysis.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated cost amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, AED).']
  current_approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual currently responsible for reviewing and approving the purchase requisition in the workflow sequence.']
  delivery_location varchar [note: 'Site address, warehouse, or specific location where the materials or equipment should be delivered. Critical for logistics planning and site coordination.']
  estimated_total_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total estimated cost for the requisition line, calculated as quantity multiplied by estimated unit cost. Used for budget availability verification.']
  estimated_unit_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated cost per unit of the requested material or service, used for budget checking and preliminary cost estimation.']
  justification_notes varchar [note: 'Business justification and rationale for the purchase requisition, including project need, schedule impact, and any special circumstances requiring expedited processing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the purchase requisition record was last updated, capturing any changes to status, approvals, or requisition details.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the material, equipment, or service being requested. Includes specifications, grade, dimensions, and any special requirements.']
  material_group varchar [note: 'Classification code grouping similar materials or services for procurement strategy, vendor assignment, and reporting (e.g., concrete, steel, electrical, MEP services).']
  pr_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the purchase requisition, externally visible and used in procurement workflows and communications.']
  pr_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the purchase requisition in the approval and conversion workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|rejected|converted|cancelled|closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pr_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the purchase requisition based on procurement category: standard materials, subcontract services, general services, stock transfers between sites, consignment arrangements, or equipment rental.']
  procurement_strategy varchar [note: 'Sourcing approach to be used for fulfilling this requisition based on value, complexity, vendor availability, and time constraints.']
  quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of the material or service being requested, expressed in the unit of measure specified.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase requisition was rejected by an approver in the workflow, if applicable.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the approver for rejecting the purchase requisition, including corrective actions required for resubmission.']
  requester_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the requester for communication regarding the purchase requisition status, clarifications, and approvals.']
  requester_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who created and submitted the purchase requisition.']
  requesting_department varchar [note: 'Name or code of the department or functional unit that originated the purchase requisition (e.g., Site Operations, Engineering, Procurement, MEP).']
  required_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the requested materials, equipment, or services must be delivered to the project site or requesting location to meet project schedule requirements.']
  requisition_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase requisition was created and submitted by the requesting party. Principal business event timestamp for the requisition.']
  technical_specification_attached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether detailed technical specifications, drawings, or engineering documents are attached to support the requisition evaluation and vendor quotation process.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit in which the requested quantity is measured (e.g., EA for each, M for meter, M3 for cubic meter, KG for kilogram, HR for hour). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|M|M2|M3|KG|TON|L|HR|DAY|SET|LOT — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  urgency_classification varchar [note: 'Priority level of the purchase requisition based on project schedule impact and delivery timeline requirements. Emergency requisitions bypass standard approval workflows.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'Specific WBS element within the project structure to which this requisition is charged. Enables granular cost control and tracking at the work package level.']

  Note: 'Internal purchase requisition raised by project or site teams to initiate procurement of materials, equipment, or services. Captures requisition number, requesting department, project and WBS element, material or service description, required quantity, unit of measure, required delivery date, estimated cost, budget availability flag, approval workflow status (pending, approved, rejected), approver, urgency classification, and conversion status (converted to RFQ or PO). Sourced from SAP MM PR process. Entry point for all formal procurement activity.'
}

Table procurement.vendor_invoice {
  vendor_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor invoice record. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Ensures invoice verification against contract terms, supporting audit of payments versus agreed contract values.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Invoices for equipment purchases/rentals must link to the asset to close the financial‑asset loop for accounting and warranty tracking.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project to which this invoice is allocated. Used for project cost tracking and job costing.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center to which this invoice is charged for financial accounting and cost control purposes.']
  finance_cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Vendor invoices need a cost‑code reference to map spend to project cost structures and support earned‑value analysis.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the general ledger account to which this invoice is posted in the financial accounting system.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Vendor invoices in construction procurement are validated via 3-way match: Purchase Order → Goods Receipt → Invoice. The existing goods_receipt_number (STRING) on vendor_invoice is a denormalized docu']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.invoice. Business justification: Vendor invoices (procurement) are matched to AP invoice records (finance) in the three-way match process. Linking enables AP reconciliation, duplicate invoice detection, and payment processing workflo']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Vendor invoice posting generates an AP journal entry (liability posting). Proper FK replaces denormalized fi_document_number text field, enabling AP posting audit trail, period-end accrual reporting, ']
  payment_certificate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.payment_certificate. Business justification: Construction payment certification drives vendor invoice approval. Linking vendor_invoice to payment_certificate enables the three-way match (PO → payment certificate → invoice) mandated by constructi']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order against which this invoice was received. Used for three-way match validation (PO-GR-Invoice).']
  rental_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.rental_agreement. Business justification: Rental invoices from equipment vendors must be matched against the rental agreement for rate validation and payment approval. \'Rental invoice three-way match\' (rental_agreement + equipment hours logge']
  subcontract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.subcontract. Business justification: Vendor invoices for subcontract work must be reconciled at subcontract level, not just head-contract level. vendor_invoice already has agreement_id (head contract) but subcontract-level invoice tracki']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor who issued this invoice. Links to vendor master data.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the invoice was approved for payment by the authorized approver.']
  blocked_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the invoice is blocked from payment, such as pending approval, missing documentation, price variance, or quality hold.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which the invoice is denominated.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount amount applied to the invoice, including early payment discounts, volume discounts, or negotiated rebates.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this invoice is currently under dispute due to discrepancies in quantity, quality, pricing, or terms.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the reason for the invoice dispute, such as quantity mismatch, pricing error, quality issues, or missing documentation.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year in which this invoice is recorded.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this invoice is recorded for financial reporting purposes.']
  invoice_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'The date the vendor issued the invoice. This is the principal business event timestamp for the invoice transaction.']
  invoice_description decimal(18,2) [note: 'Textual description of the goods or services covered by this invoice, providing context for the transaction.']
  invoice_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total invoice amount before taxes and adjustments. Represents the base value of goods or services invoiced.']
  invoice_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total invoice amount payable after applying taxes, discounts, and adjustments. This is the final amount due to the vendor.']
  invoice_received_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'The date the invoice was received by the procurement or accounts payable department.']
  invoice_status decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor invoice in the verification and payment workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|received|under_review|blocked|approved|posted|paid|disputed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_type decimal(18,2) [note: 'Classification of the invoice document type indicating the nature of the transaction (standard invoice, credit memo for returns, debit memo for additional charges, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|credit_memo|debit_memo|prepayment|final|progress|retention_release — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments related to this invoice, such as special instructions, clarifications, or internal remarks.']
  payment_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual date on which payment was made to the vendor for this invoice.']
  payment_due_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'The date by which payment must be made to the vendor to comply with payment terms and avoid late payment penalties.']
  payment_method decimal(18,2) [note: 'The method by which payment will be made to the vendor for this invoice.']
  payment_reference_number decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reference number of the payment transaction when the invoice is paid, linking the invoice to the payment record.']
  payment_terms decimal(18,2) [note: 'The agreed payment terms for this invoice, such as Net 30, Net 60, or 2/10 Net 30, defining the payment schedule and any early payment discount conditions.']
  retention_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount withheld from the invoice payment as retention per contract terms, typically released upon project completion or after the Defects Liability Period (DLP).']
  retention_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the invoice amount withheld as retention, typically ranging from 5% to 10% per construction contract terms.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the invoice, including sales tax, VAT, GST, or other applicable taxes.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax code applied to this invoice, defining the tax rate and tax type (VAT, GST, sales tax) per jurisdiction.']
  three_way_match_status varchar [note: 'Result of the three-way match validation comparing purchase order, goods receipt (GR), and invoice. Indicates whether quantities, prices, and terms align across all three documents.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the invoice verification process indicating whether the invoice has been validated against the purchase order and goods receipt.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of withholding tax deducted from the invoice payment per tax regulations, to be remitted to tax authorities.']

  Note: 'Vendor invoices received against purchase orders capturing invoice number, vendor, PO reference, invoice date, invoice amount, tax amount, currency, payment due date, three-way match status (PO-GR-Invoice), invoice verification status (blocked, approved, posted), SAP FI document number, and dispute flag. Distinct from client billing invoices owned by the finance domain — this is the SSOT for vendor payable documents in procurement.'
}

Table procurement.sourcing_info_record {
  sourcing_info_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the sourcing_info_record association']
  material_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the material catalog entry for which these vendor-specific sourcing terms apply.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor master record that is approved to supply the material under these sourcing terms.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this vendor-material sourcing record is valid and the vendor is eligible to supply this material at the stated terms. Used for time-bounded source determination during PO creation.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which this vendor-material sourcing record is no longer valid. Expired records are retained for historical pricing and audit purposes but excluded from active source determination.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Vendor-specific number of calendar days from PO issuance to material delivery for this vendor-material combination. Overrides the standard procurement_lead_time_days on material_catalog when this sourcing record is active. Cannot reside on material_catalog (varies by vendor) or vendor (varies by material).']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal [note: 'Vendor-specific minimum quantity that must be ordered for this material in the material\'s base unit of measure. Overrides the standard minimum_order_quantity on material_catalog when this sourcing record is active. Varies per vendor-material combination.']
  preferred_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor is the preferred (primary) source for this material. When true, the procurement system defaults to this vendor during automatic source determination. Only one sourcing record per material should carry preferred_flag = true at any given time.']
  price_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the negotiated unit_price. Defaults to the vendor\'s currency_code but may differ for international sourcing agreements.']
  price_unit_quantity decimal [note: 'The quantity basis for the unit_price (e.g., price per 100 units, price per 1 tonne). Required to correctly compute PO line item costs when the pricing unit differs from the order unit of measure.']
  sourcing_info_record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this vendor-material sourcing record. ACTIVE = currently valid for PO creation; EXPIRED = past expiry_date; SUSPENDED = temporarily blocked (e.g., vendor compliance issue); PENDING_APPROVAL = awaiting procurement approval before activation.']
  unit_price decimal [note: 'Vendor-negotiated unit price for this material in the vendor\'s transaction currency. Varies per vendor-material combination and cannot reside on either vendor or material_catalog alone. Used as the default price during PO creation in SAP MM.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Contract/Agreement between vendor and material_catalog in the procurement domain. It captures the approved sourcing terms under which a specific vendor may supply a specific material, including negotiated price, vendor-specific lead time, minimum order quantity, preferred source flag, and validity period. Each record links one vendor to one material_catalog entry and carries attributes that exist only in the context of this vendor-material sourcing relationship. Equivalent to the SAP MM Purchasing Info Record (PIR) and serves as the authoritative source for vendor-material pricing and sourcing eligibility used during PO creation and MTO processes.. Existence Justification: In construction procurement, a vendor can supply multiple materials from the catalog, and a single material can be sourced from multiple vendors — each at different unit prices, lead times, minimum order quantities, and validity periods. This is the SAP MM Purchasing Info Record concept, a well-established operational business entity that procurement teams actively create, maintain, and expire as sourcing conditions change. The relationship is not derivable from transactional data; it is a standing master data record that governs which vendor-material combinations are approved for purchasing and under what commercial terms.'
}

Table material.master {
  master_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for master']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Inventory valuation process: each material master maps to a GL account (e.g., raw materials inventory asset account) for balance sheet posting and stock valuation reporting. Standard construction ERP ']
  substitution_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of an approved substitute material.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary supplier for this material.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the material record was approved for use.']
  conformance_certificate_date date [note: 'Date the compliance certificate was issued.']
  conformance_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the material’s compliance certificate (e.g., ISO, ASTM).']
  cost_per_unit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard cost of one unit of material in the specified currency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the material master record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for cost fields.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the material, including typical applications.']
  dimensions_cm varchar [note: 'Length×Width×Height dimensions in centimeters, formatted as LxWxH.']
  expiration_date decimal(9,4) [note: 'Calendar date when the material batch expires.']
  hazardous_material_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the material is classified as hazardous under OSHA/EPA regulations.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days from order to receipt.']
  leeds_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is eligible for LEED credit consideration.']
  leeds_credit_applicability varchar [note: 'LEED credit categories the material can contribute to.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the material record.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier used for quality control and recall management.']
  manufacturer_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the manufacturer’s country.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactures the material.']
  material_code varchar [note: 'Enterprise‑wide unique code or catalogue number for the material.']
  material_origin_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the material\'s source.']
  max_order_qty varchar [note: 'Largest quantity that can be ordered in a single purchase.']
  min_order_qty varchar [note: 'Smallest quantity that can be ordered from the supplier.']
  minimum_performance_criteria varchar [note: 'Minimum performance thresholds the material must meet (e.g., compressive strength).']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the material.']
  reorder_point_qty varchar [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a replenishment order.']
  safety_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Buffer stock kept to protect against demand variability.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the material can be stored before it expires.']
  specification_grade varchar [note: 'Grade or class of the material as defined by the governing standard.']
  specification_standard varchar [note: 'Reference standard governing the material (e.g., ASTM, BS, ISO, ACI, AISC).']
  storage_requirements varchar [note: 'Special storage conditions (e.g., temperature, humidity, segregation).']
  substitution_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the client/engineer approval document for the substitution.']
  substitution_reason varchar [note: 'Engineering or client‑approved justification for the substitution.']
  supplier_part_number varchar [note: 'Supplier‑assigned part number for the material.']
  technical_specification varchar [note: 'Full technical specification document reference or summary.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for inventory and consumption tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the material record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the material record for change control.']
  volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Physical volume of a single unit of material in cubic meters.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net weight of a single unit of material in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Authoritative master record for all construction materials and consumables used across projects. Captures material identity, detailed technical specifications (applicable standards — ASTM, BS, ISO, ACI, AISC — grade/class, minimum performance criteria, specification code, specification revision), classification (concrete, steel, MEP components, aggregates, timber, formwork, etc.), unit of measure, approved equivalent substitutes with engineering justification, LEED compliance flags, LEED credit applicability, hazardous material indicators, shelf life, storage requirements, and supplier cross-references. Manages specification lifecycle (draft, approved, superseded) and material qualification status. Records approved substitutions with reason, client/engineer approval reference, cost impact, and affected BOQ lines. This is the SSOT for material identity, technical specifications, and approved substitutes in the construction enterprise — all transactions reference this record.'
}

Table material.warehouse {
  warehouse_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the warehouse record.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: REQUIRED: Some warehouses are owned or leased by a client; the FK enables asset ownership and liability tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Warehouse overhead allocation: construction warehouses are assigned to cost centers to track storage costs (utilities, security, handling). Required for overhead cost allocation reports and cost cente']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction site or project to which the warehouse is attached.']
  access_control_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to control entry to the warehouse.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the warehouse.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, unit, etc.).']
  capacity_area_sqm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum usable floor area in square metres.']
  capacity_weight_tonnes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum total weight the warehouse can safely store, expressed in metric tonnes.']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the hazardous‑material certification expires.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the warehouse is located.']
  climate_control_type varchar [note: 'Specific climate control method applied when temperature_controlled is true.']
  code varchar [note: 'Business‑visible alphanumeric code used to reference the warehouse in external systems.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the warehouse location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the warehouse record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the warehouse, its purpose, and special characteristics.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the warehouse became operational or was officially opened.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the warehouse ceased operations or is scheduled for closure (null if still active).']
  gps_accuracy_meters varchar [note: 'Estimated positional accuracy of the GPS coordinates, in metres.']
  gps_latitude double [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the warehouse centre point.']
  gps_longitude double [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the warehouse centre point.']
  hazardous_material_certification_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of hazardous‑material certification held, if any.']
  inventory_management_system varchar [note: 'Name of the downstream system that tracks inventory within this warehouse (e.g., SAP WM, Procore).']
  is_hazardous_material_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warehouse holds a valid hazardous‑material storage certification.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety or compliance inspection.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the warehouse or laydown yard.']
  next_inspection_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next required inspection.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form remarks or operational comments.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard daily operating window (e.g., 08:00‑17:00).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the warehouse address.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Overall security classification of the warehouse.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the warehouse location.']
  storage_zone_details varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of internal zones, racks, bins, or yard sections.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'True if the warehouse maintains a controlled temperature environment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the warehouse record.']
  warehouse_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the warehouse.']
  warehouse_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the warehouse based on construction and regulatory characteristics.']

  Note: 'Master record for physical warehouse, laydown yard, and storage locations across construction sites and central depots. Tracks warehouse type (bonded, open yard, climate-controlled, hazmat-certified), capacity (area sqm, weight tonnes), GPS coordinates, site association, custodian, operating hours, hazardous material storage certification, and granular storage locations (rack positions, bin rows, open yard zones). Supports multi-site inventory management, material transfer logistics, and FIFO/FEFO stock management for batch-tracked materials.'
}

Table material.stock_level {
  stock_level_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each stock level record.']
  batch_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to material.batch_lot. Business justification: stock_level tracks on-hand inventory position which can be batch/lot specific (e.g., 50 units of concrete batch B-2024-001 at warehouse W1). The batch_number and lot_number on stock_level are denormal']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Inventory balance sheet reporting: each stock location maps to a GL inventory account for on-hand stock valuation. Construction finance requires stock-level GL account assignment to produce accurate b']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to material.material_master']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'FK to material.warehouse']
  blocked_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity held due to quality issues, holds, or regulatory restrictions.']
  committed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity allocated to specific construction activities or contracts.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard cost of a single unit of material in the base currency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the stock level record was first created.']
  expiration_date decimal(9,4) [note: 'Date after which the material is considered unusable or non‑compliant.']
  in_transit_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity that has been dispatched but not yet received at the location.']
  last_movement_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent stock transaction affecting this record.']
  last_movement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the most recent stock transaction.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the stock level record.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or storage site where the stock resides.']
  material_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier or catalogue number for the material.']
  max_stock_level decimal(18,3) [note: 'Upper threshold for inventory to avoid over‑stocking.']
  min_stock_level decimal(18,3) [note: 'Lowest permissible inventory level before stock is considered understocked.']
  quality_inspection_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity currently undergoing quality inspection or testing.']
  quantity_on_hand decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total amount of material physically present in the location.']
  reorder_point decimal(18,3) [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a replenishment request.']
  reserved_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity earmarked for pending work packages or purchase orders.']
  safety_stock decimal(18,3) [note: 'Buffer quantity maintained to protect against demand variability or supply delays.']
  stock_level_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stock quantity.']
  supplier_code varchar [note: 'External identifier of the supplier providing the material.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity fields.']

  Note: 'Current on-hand inventory position for each material at each warehouse/storage location. Captures unrestricted stock, quality-inspection stock, blocked stock, reserved stock, in-transit quantities, and committed (reserved-for-activity) quantities. Tracks reorder point, minimum/maximum stock levels, safety stock thresholds, and last movement date. Updated in real-time by stock movements (receipts, issues, transfers, returns). Critical for BOQ reconciliation, preventing site stoppages due to material shortages, and supporting procurement trigger automation.'
}

  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Stock movement records must be linked to the originating purchase order for audit trails, cost allocation, and receipt verification.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of materials physically received at site or warehouse against a Purchase Order (PO). Captures GR date, PO reference, delivery note number, supplier, material, quantity received, unit of measure, batch/lot number, storage location, receiving inspector, and GR status (partial, complete, over-delivery). Triggers quality inspection workflow and updates stock levels. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA MIGO GR posting and Procore material delivery logs.'
}

  cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Batch-level job costing: in construction, specific material batches (concrete pours, structural steel lots) are tracked to cost codes for precise cost allocation and compliance reporting. Required for']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to material.master. Business justification: batch_lot is the traceability master for a specific batch/lot of a material — it must reference which material master it belongs to. Currently batch_lot has material_description as a denormalized stri']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: A batch/lot in construction is sourced against a specific PO. Linking batch_lot to purchase_order enables full procurement-to-inventory traceability — essential for warranty claims, quality disputes w']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the warehouse holding the material.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned batch number used to identify a specific production run.']
  batch_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the batch/lot.']
  certificate_of_conformance_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number of the certificate confirming material meets specifications.']
  cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Acquisition cost of the batch/lot.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch/lot record was first created in the system.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the cost.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for disposing of excess or waste material.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the material should not be used.']
  inspection_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the last inspection met acceptance criteria.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent quality or safety inspection.']

  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that produced the material.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Category of material represented by the batch/lot.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks or observations.']
  production_date date [note: 'Date the material batch was manufactured.']
  quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Amount of material in the batch expressed in the unit of measure.']
  quarantine_status varchar [note: 'Current quarantine state of the batch/lot.']

  unit_weight decimal(12,3) [note: 'Weight per unit of material (e.g., kg per piece).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the batch/lot record.']

  Note: 'Batch and lot traceability master for materials requiring full chain-of-custody tracking — concrete pours (batch plant ticket), structural steel heats, rebar coils, MEP cable drums, and chemical admixtures. Captures batch number, lot number, manufacturer, production date, expiry date, certificate of conformance reference, test results summary, quarantine status, and linked goods receipt. Supports FAT/SAT traceability and NCR investigations.'
}

Required for cost center budget vs. actual material cost variance ']
  finance_cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Required for BOQ Budgeting: each BOQ line is associated with a cost code to track planned vs. actual costs.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material or item that this BOQ line represents.']
  project_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.project_budget. Business justification: BOQ-to-budget linkage: material BOQ lines are the basis for project budget line items. Direct link supports budget vs. actual material cost reporting, change order impact analysis, and earned value me']
  technical_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.technical_specification. Business justification: BOQ line items are governed by technical specifications that define material standards, grades, and workmanship requirements. BOQ preparation and specification compliance audits require direct linkage']
  contract_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material that is contractually committed in the BOQ.']
  is_change_order boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line originates from a change order (true) or the original BOQ (false).']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the material or work item represented by the line.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the BOQ, used for sorting and display.']
  material_boq_line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the BOQ line.']

  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the BOQ line record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the BOQ line record.']

  total_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated line value (contract_quantity × unit_rate) in the project currency.']
  trade_discipline varchar [note: 'Construction trade or discipline (e.g., concrete, steel, MEP) associated with the line.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the quantity (e.g., m3, ton, pcs).']
  unit_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed price per unit of measure for the material.']

  Note: 'Bill of Quantities (BOQ) line item representing material and work quantities baselined for a project, used as the material domain\'s reference for take-off reconciliation, consumption tracking, and procurement planning. Captures BOQ item code, description, unit of measure, contract quantity, unit rate, total value, WBS element, trade/discipline, and revision history. While contractual BOQ ownership may reside in the contract domain, this product serves as the material domain\'s operational view for MTO reconciliation, material consumption variance analysis, and progress measurement against planned quantities.'
}

Table material.mto_line {
  mto_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for mto_line']
  bim_model_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the BIM model version containing the material definition.']

BOQ (Bill of Quantities) lines represent the baselined planned quantities for a pr']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project associated with this MTO line.']
  contract_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_milestone. Business justification: MTO required_by_date is driven by contract milestones — materials must arrive before milestone completion. Procurement planners link MTO lines to contract milestones for critical-path material schedul']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for MTO Cost Planning: material take‑off quantities are allocated to cost centers for cost estimation and forecasting.']
  cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: MTO procurement cost coding: every material take-off line must be assigned a cost code for procurement cost tracking and committed cost reporting. Construction cost engineers use this link to track ma']
  drawing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.drawing. Business justification: MTO lines are extracted directly from construction drawings — the Material Take-Off process requires traceability from each quantity line back to its source drawing. mto_line.drawing_number is a denor']
  maintenance_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.maintenance_order. Business justification: Major equipment overhauls generate MTO lines for spare parts procurement. Linking mto_line to the triggering maintenance_order enables procurement planning driven by maintenance schedules — a standard']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record representing the specific material.']
  mto_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent MTO document to which this line belongs.']
  project_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.project_budget. Business justification: MTO budget consumption tracking: MTO lines represent material procurement commitments that consume project budget. Direct link enables committed cost vs. budget reporting and budget sufficiency checks']
  actual_received_date date [note: 'Date when the material was received on site.']
  actual_received_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of material actually received on site.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the MTO line.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the MTO line was approved.']
  compliance_certification varchar [note: 'Applicable certification or standard (e.g., ISO9001, ASTM) that the material meets.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the MTO line record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for cost estimates.']
  design_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material calculated from design drawings or BIM models.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'Engineering discipline associated with the material.']
  estimated_unit_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost per unit of material for procurement planning.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the material is no longer usable or compliant.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is critical to project schedule or safety.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the record has been logically deleted.']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is classified as hazardous.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of days from order to delivery for the material.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the MTO header.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for traceability of the material.']
  material_specification varchar [note: 'Detailed specification or grade of the material as defined in the BOQ.']
  mto_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MTO line.']
  net_required_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total material quantity required after applying wastage factor.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or remarks about the MTO line.']
  procurement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the procurement process for this material line.']
  quantity_uom_factor decimal(12,4) [note: 'Factor to convert between alternative units of measure for the material.']
  required_by_date date [note: 'Date by which the material must be on site to meet construction schedule.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision identifier of the drawing or BIM model for this line.']
  safety_data_sheet_url varchar [note: 'Link to the safety data sheet document for the material.']
  site_location_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the construction site or warehouse where material will be used or stored.']
  supplier_preferred varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the preferred supplier for this material.']
  total_estimated_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total estimated cost calculated as net_required_quantity multiplied by estimated_unit_price.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for the material quantities.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the MTO line record.']
  variance_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Difference between total_estimated_cost and actual cost incurred.']
  variance_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Difference between net_required_quantity and actual_received_quantity.']
  wastage_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage factor applied to account for material loss during construction.']

  Note: 'Material Take-Off (MTO) line item derived from engineering drawings and BIM models, representing the estimated material quantities required for construction. Captures MTO item, material master reference, design quantity, wastage factor, net required quantity, unit of measure, drawing/BIM model reference, discipline (civil, structural, MEP), revision number, and MTO status (draft, approved, issued-for-procurement). Bridges design domain and procurement/inventory planning.'
}

Table material.mto_header {
  mto_header_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for mto_header']
  cash_flow_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cash_flow_forecast. Direct link to cash_flow_forecast (which has material_procurement_amount field) enables accurate cash flow for']

While mto_line has construction_project_id, the header itself lacks a direct project FK, preventing project-level MTO register repor']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: MTO header cost center assignment: material transfer/procurement events are assigned to cost centers for overhead and procurement cost allocation. Required for cost center actual cost reporting and pr']
  parent_mto_header_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on mto_header (parent_mto_header_id)']
  project_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.project_budget. Business justification: MTO header budget commitment: bulk material procurement events (MTO headers) create budget commitments. Direct link to project_budget supports commitment accounting, budget availability checks, and ca']
  project_site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the site or warehouse where materials are shipped from.']
  requesting_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal or external party that requested the material transfer.']
  subcontract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.subcontract. Business justification: Subcontract packages in construction generate specific MTO packages for subcontractor-supplied materials. Project controls teams track material take-offs per subcontract for procurement planning and s']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the material transfer order was approved.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production batch associated with the transferred materials.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance state of the material transfer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this MTO header record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code representing the currency of total_cost.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'External document or certificate identifier linked to the transfer (e.g., conformance certificate).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the material transfer was initiated or recorded.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transfer is considered critical for project schedule.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for traceability of concrete, steel, or other consumables.']
